title: DQ.12 — Settings drawer form for fleet-wide default disk quota
description: DQ.12 was marked complete-but-deferred ('field editable via PATCH /ui/api/settings; React form lands when settings drawer gets a build-cache section'). For 1.7.0 this is too hidden — the changelog will tell users about disk quota but they can't change the fleet default without curl. Add a number input to SettingsDrawer.tsx for default_worker_disk_quota_bytes (GiB units, range 1..1024 to match backend validator), grouped near firmware_cache_max_gb / firmware_retention_days under Disk management.
notes: Pulled forward into 1.7.0 during release-prep DQ live verification.
close_reason: Landed in 1.7.0-dev.70 (pulled forward from deferred state)

title: Worker boots over quota silently — first-heartbeat sweep missing
description: DQ.9's _run_disk_quota_sweep has three trigger sites (startup / post-job / quota-lowered). The cold-start path fails: startup short-circuits because env-var quota is None, then the first heartbeat transition None→10 GiB doesn't satisfy 'prev_quota is not None and new_quota < prev_quota', so no sweep fires. Workers that boot over budget stay over budget until their next compile. Live evidence on hass-4 dev.69: LENOVO-1 at 25.75/10 GiB (+158%), OPTIPLEX-7 31.25/10 GiB (+212%), docker-optiplex-5 27.81/10 GiB (+178%), all freed=0. Fix: on the None→quota first-heartbeat transition, fire a sweep labelled 'initial-quota'.
notes: Found during 1.7.0 release prep DQ live verification on hass-4 dev.69, 2026-05-02.

title: 1.7.0 — heffneil-inspired DM + tags & routing (release epic)
description:
Theme: heffneil-inspired device-management polish + fleet tags & routing. Adds three pieces of per-device UX inspired by `heffneil/esphome-enhanced-dashboard` (`dev-plans/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_heffneil_enhanced_dashboard.md` — mark-inactive to skip polling on known-offline devices, ping diagnostic, install-to-specific-address for recovery flows), ships user-managed tags on devices + workers plus a global K8s-flavored rule engine that decides which workers can claim which jobs, and continues the ESPHome-delegation cleanup. Renamed from 1.6.3 to 1.7.0 because the scope crosses the patch/minor line — these are user-visible features, not hardening.

The Gold-tier-flip work that 1.6.2 pivoted away from originally rode along here too. In practice the tags + routing piece consumed the bandwidth, so the tier-flip plus its dependent QS / HT / CI / SD items moved to `dev-plans/WORKITEMS-1.7.1.md` mid-cycle. The four QS / HT / CI items that landed before the slide (HT.5, HT.6, CI.3, CI.5) plus QS.G10's WONTFIX disposition stay below as the historical record of what shipped under 1.7.0 dev tags.

Source: dev-plans/WORKITEMS-1.7.0.md

title: 1.7.1 — Honest Gold (release epic)
description:
Theme: Honest Gold — the quality-scale tier flip 1.7.0 punted on. 1.7.0 carried 'Honest Gold' as a workstream alongside heffneil-inspired device-management polish and fleet tags + routing; in practice the tags + routing piece consumed the bandwidth, and the tier-flip slid here. The headline user-visible change is manifest.quality_scale: silver → gold — every QS / HT / TP / CI / SD workitem below exists in support of that one line being honest at tag time. Two ESPHome-delegation items (EH.3 / EH.4) also carried over from 1.7.0 as insurance + perf nits — both pure internal cleanup, neither user-visible.

Definition of 'Gold' for a custom integration: hassfest never runs on out-of-tree code in prod, so 'official' Gold isn't available. Gold-equivalent means: (i) every rule in Bronze+Silver+Gold of script/hassfest/quality_scale.py's ALL_RULES is done or exempt with a reason in our quality_scale.yaml; (ii) a local python3 -m script.hassfest --action quality_scale against our integration passes clean when the manifest claims gold; (iii) CI runs that same validator on every PR (CI.4) so the claim can't silently rot. That's the bar this release targets.

Source: dev-plans/WORKITEMS-1.7.1.md

title: SD.3 Produce TEST-AUDIT-1.7.1.md as the last workitem before tag
description: Prove each TEST-AUDIT-1.6.1 top blind spot has durable closure (a test exists AND would fail without the fix AND the underlying bug class is structurally prevented, not just patched). For each of items 1-13 in TEST-AUDIT-1.6.1, write one line: 'closed via HT.X (1.6.2 / 1.7.0 / 1.7.1)' or 're-deferred — here's why and here's the owning workitem in 1.8.' If even one entry reads 'we ran out of time,' treat that as a signal to cut non-blocking scope and land the test. Audit the audit. Also produce TEST-AUDIT-1.7.0.md covering tags + routing + DM + RC if 1.7.0 ships without one — defer to whichever turn closes the 1.7.0 release.

title: SD.2 Release-blocker gate pre-tag (Gold-grade)
description: Before tagging v1.7.1, every one of the following must be true: (1) dev-plans/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md's security-docs cross-check passes (no stale claims). (2) python3 -m script.hassfest --action quality_scale passes clean at the tier declared in manifest.json.quality_scale. If the manifest says gold, zero errors; if every Gold rule isn't done/exempt, the manifest tier drops to whatever is honest (silver or bronze) before the release tag — we do not ship a claim hassfest doesn't back. (3) The TEST-AUDIT-1.6.1 Top-5 blind spots (HT.2-HT.4 here, plus HT.1 in 1.6.2 and HT.5 in 1.7.0) have landed. Not in progress, not partially. Landed + merged + CI-green. (4) brands PR at home-assistant/brands is either merged (so brands can be done) or the quality_scale.yaml:brands comment carries the open PR URL and tier drops if it was gating Gold. (5) scripts/check-invariants.sh — all rules (PY-1..10, PY-10b, HT.2's reseed-consumer rule, UI-1..7, E2E-1) green. (6) HT.12's coverage number ≥95% for ha-addon/custom_integration/esphome_fleet/**. (7) ha-addon/CHANGELOG.md accurately describes what users see changing from 1.7.0 → 1.7.1 (the tier flip, translated entity names, runtime-data migration, the new docs sections, repair-issues surfacing). Per CLAUDE.md: only changes relative to 1.7.0; the QS.G10 attempt+revert from 1.7.0-dev never existed from the user's perspective.

title: CI.6 Coverage ratchet for the integration
description: Add a job step that runs pytest --cov=ha-addon/custom_integration/esphome_fleet --cov-report=term --cov-fail-under=95 (HT.12) and fails if the number drops below the committed threshold. Store the threshold in a single place (env var or a pytest config key) so bumping it post-HT.1 is one line. Keeps Gold's test-coverage claim honest between releases.

title: CI.4 Hassfest runs the quality-scale validator at our claimed tier
description: .github/workflows/hassfest.yml today validates manifest shape only. Adjust the action inputs (or run python3 -m script.hassfest --action quality_scale directly against a checkout of home-assistant/core) so the committed quality_scale.yaml gets validated against manifest.json.quality_scale's claimed tier on every PR. Validators that fire at Gold (from script/hassfest/quality_scale_validation/): action_setup.py, config_entry_unloading.py, config_flow.py, diagnostics.py, discovery.py, parallel_updates.py, reauthentication_flow.py, reconfiguration_flow.py, runtime_data.py, test_before_setup.py, unique_config_entry.py. Without this gate, TP.3's tier-flip is a file that could silently rot.

title: TP.3 (clauses c+d) — flip manifest.quality_scale to gold only when honest
description: (c) Every Bronze+Silver+Gold rule from script/hassfest/quality_scale.py's ALL_RULES must appear with done/exempt in our quality_scale.yaml — any rule still todo must be closed by a QS.* workitem above or re-scoped to a future release (and the manifest tier drops accordingly). (d) After every QS.* item lands, run python3 -m script.hassfest --action quality_scale locally; expect zero errors at tier gold. Only then edit manifest.json from quality_scale: bronze → quality_scale: gold. Ground rule: if even one Gold rule stays todo at ship-time, manifest stays at silver or bronze — we do not ship a claim hassfest doesn't back. THIS IS THE SINGLE DELIVERY THIS RELEASE EXISTS TO MAKE.

title: #224 CI test job red on PyYAML build failure
description: CI test job has been red on every push since 2026-05-01 with "ERROR: Failed to build 'PyYAML' when getting requirements to build wheel" deep inside pip's resolver. Root cause: aioesphomeapi 44.23.0 was published 2026-04-30T04:33:44Z, ~50 min after our last green CI. esphome 2026.4.3 pins aioesphomeapi to 44.16.1 exactly. The CI install step listed both aioesphomeapi and esphome unconstrained in a single pip install, so pip resolved aioesphomeapi to the new latest (44.23.0) first, then had to backtrack through every esphome version trying to find one that accepts it — eventually hitting esphome 2023.6.5 which pins PyYAML to 6.0, an sdist-only release whose setup.py calls cython_sources and fails to build under Python 3.12 / modern Cython (AttributeError: 'build_ext' object has no attribute 'cython_sources'). Fix: split the install into two pip passes — pip install esphome first so its strict transitive pins win, then pip install pytest … aioesphomeapi …; pip without --upgrade keeps already-installed packages, so the second pass leaves aioesphomeapi at 44.16.1. Verified locally in a fresh Python 3.12 venv: pre-fix install reproduces the PyYAML 6.0 sdist build failure; post-fix install succeeds with aioesphomeapi-44.16.1 + esphome-2026.4.3 + all test extras and zero backtracking. dev.66 follow-up: with the install fixed, CI ran the full suite and surfaced one masked-by-the-install-failure regression: test_claim_job_disk_blocked_worker_returns_204 (added in #219) wraps the first claim attempt (which expects 204) but not the second claim attempt (which expects 200). The success-path claim hits the real create_bundle_async, which tries to read device.yaml from a tmp_path that doesn't contain it, raising FileNotFoundError → 500. Fix: wrap the second ta.get(...) in the same `with patch("api.create_bundle_async", new=AsyncMock(return_value=_make_test_bundle())):` block the rest of test_api.py's success-path claims use. Verified locally: 69/69 in test_api.py pass.

title: #223 Investigate client on docker-pve dying
description: Looks like the client on docker-pve died. Investigate what happened there.

title: #222 Tag-filter pill bar flipped to AND-logic
description: Tag-filter pill bar on the Devices and Workers tabs flipped from OR-logic to AND-logic. Pre-fix, clicking kitchen then prod showed every device tagged kitchen plus every device tagged prod (the union); the user's mental model was the intersection — "narrow this to kitchen-AND-prod devices." The new behaviour matches: clicking each successive pill narrows the visible set. Code-side, both DevicesTab.filteredVisible and WorkersTab.filteredWorkers swapped tagsOnRow.some(t => selectedSet.has(t)) for selectedTags.every(t => tagsOnRowSet.has(t)). TagFilterBar.tsx docstring rewritten to call out the AND semantics. New regression spec e2e/tag-filter-and.spec.ts clicks kitchen (both kitchen-tagged fixtures visible), then clicks cosy (only Living Room Sensor — which carries both kitchen + cosy — survives), proving the second click narrowed rather than widened the set.

title: #221 Queue-tab row actions reshaped: Rerun · Clear · Log inline
description: Queue-tab row actions reshaped to the user's mental model. Inline buttons are now Rerun · Clear · Log (Log was hamburgered between #209 and #221; bringing it back as a peer of Rerun/Clear matches the frequency with which users open logs to investigate failures or successes — burying it behind the hamburger added friction with no real benefit). The hamburger keeps Cancel (under "Job", destructive — shouldn't be a one-click action on every in-progress row), the Download firmware group when applicable, and the full Device group via the shared DeviceMenuSection. Edit YAML moved out of the "Job" group and into the bottom of the Device group (closes #216) — Edit YAML is conceptually a device-targeted action, and grouping it with Live Logs / Restart / Ping / Install to address makes the hamburger read coherently. DeviceMenuSection gained an optional onEdit prop so the Devices tab (which has its own inline Edit button) doesn't duplicate the entry. When the row's target was deleted from the config dir, Edit YAML falls back to a "Job" group entry so the user can still open the editor (the file might be restorable from .archive/). Existing e2e tests that drove the hamburger ("View log" → click) updated to drive the inline Log button instead; all 188 mocked Playwright specs stay green against the new layout. dev.64 follow-up: the live e2e-hass-4/cyd-office-info.spec.ts was missed in the dev.63 push (still drove getByRole('menuitem', {name:'View log'})); fixed in dev.64. Subsequent hass-4 smoke retries on dev.64 hit an unrelated environmental block — the cyd-on-mac routing rule routes cyd-tagged compiles to macdaddy.localdomain, which was busy compiling another fleet target during the smoke window — so the "compile runs to completion" step waited the full COMPILE_BUDGET_MS budget for an idle macos worker that never freed up. Not a regression in #221; the inline Log button was clicked successfully and the modal opened. Mocked specs (188/188) prove the menu layout end-to-end without depending on a live worker pool.

title: #220 broadened #214 self-heal to all corrupted pio-slot symptoms
description: broadened #214's mid-run self-heal to cover every flavor of corrupted pio-slot-N/ that surfaces in the home lab, not just cc1: posix_spawnp ENOENT. Fresh repro: a 21-job batch on hass-4 (2026-05-01) with failures spread across 4 workers (AI-MacBook-Pro.localdomain × 16, docker-optiplex-5 × 3, macdaddy.localdomain × 1, LENOVO-1 × 1) and 4 distinct symptoms — Missing framework-arduinoespressif32-libs package (the framework-libs dir is present but only carries metadata files, no library blobs), tool-esptoolpy does not appear to be a Python project, as neither pyproject.toml nor setup.py are present (PIO's pip install -e <tool> against a half-extracted package), ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'esptool.__init__'; 'esptool' is not a package (#114's signature — esptool half-installed in penv/.espidf-*/), and "sh: 1: /esphome-versions/pio-slot-N/penv/bin/esptool: not found" → Error 127 (penv binary outright missing). Pre-fix #214 only matched cc1, so all four landed in plain failed with no retry. Fix: new _BROKEN_PIO_SIGNATURES regex tuple + _is_broken_pio_state(log) helper in client.py (one place to add new symptoms, replaces both inline cc1_signature checks), and _wipe_broken_toolchain now removes both pio-slot-N/packages/ and pio-slot-N/penv/ so penv-side rot is recovered too. Disk-pressure guard, dist/ preservation (so re-extract doesn't re-download), and the one-shot in-process retry are all unchanged. Tests in tests/test_client.py: 6 parametrized lab-failure log excerpts (cc1, missing framework-libs, not-a-Python-project, ModuleNotFoundError-esptool, penv/bin/...-not-found+Error-127, generic firmware.bin Error 127) all match the new helper; 5 negative cases (clean compile, user component error, OTA error, user C++ error, unrelated make Error 127) don't; and the wipe tests now assert both subtrees are removed while dist/ + appstate.json siblings survive. Closes #114 — the same self-heal mechanism handles its symptom, no upstream-PIO changes needed. Live verification (2026-05-01): re-queued the 21 originally-failed targets on the dev.62 workers — 19 passed on first attempt (most landing on the same AI-MacBook-Pro.localdomain worker that previously had a 16-fail streak; the in-process self-heal wiped the corrupted slot subtrees and the retry succeeded mid-job, no operator intervention), 2 stragglers (screek-2a-5 hit a transient "Failed to install Python dependencies into penv" from the espidf-venv bootstrap step + seplos-bms-1 lost its claimed worker mid-compile after AI-MacBook-Pro briefly went offline 3× in a row); both passed on a single re-queue. Final result 21/21 green vs. 0/21 pre-fix.

title: #219 workers self-pause when disk fills up
description: workers self-pause when their disk fills up. Live observation on hass-4 turned up docker-pve at 49 GiB / 50 GiB / 0 B free, with PackageException: Could not install package 'platformio/tool-esptoolpy' plus "Tool Manager: Warning! Package Mirror: [Errno 28] No space left on device" in 20 of 25 failed jobs in the queue. #214's mid-run toolchain self-heal can't help once the filesystem stops accepting writes — the wipe-and-reinstall hits ENOSPC at the same step. Fix: Worker.health_blocked_reason (server side, derived from each heartbeat's system_info.disk_used_pct with two-threshold hysteresis — enter at 95 %, exit at 90 %, in ha-addon/server/constants.py:WORKER_DISK_BLOCK_ENTER_PCT / WORKER_DISK_BLOCK_EXIT_PCT). The job-claim path returns 204 for a disk-blocked worker (sibling of the disabled short-circuit), and the deferral candidate pool skips it so jobs aren't stranded by being "deferred to a faster worker that won't claim." A new "disk full" badge in the WorkersTab Status column carries the explanation in its title= (UI-7-compliant icon-only-button). Belt-and-suspenders preflight in client._wipe_broken_toolchain short-circuits the #214 self-heal with one honest log line when free disk is < 1 GiB, so the worker stops looping itself into infinite ENOSPC. Tests: 4 hysteresis cases in tests/test_registry.py + a claim-refusal case in tests/test_api.py mirroring the existing disabled test. No protocol change, no MIN_IMAGE_VERSION bump — old workers benefit without redeploy because the disk-pct field has been in SystemInfo all along.

title: #218 UpgradeModal horizontal scrollbar fixed
description: UpgradeModal horizontal scrollbar fixed. Each radio row in the Action / Worker / Version sections used whitespace-nowrap to keep the short label ("Schedule Recurring", "Tag expression", etc.) from wrapping (#78), but the descriptive <span> after the label ("— compile only, no OTA; grab the .bin from the Queue tab", "— scheduler picks at compile time", etc.) was forced onto the same line and could exceed the 600 px modal width — the dialog's overflow-y-auto then surfaced a horizontal scrollbar at the bottom. Swapped each label's whitespace-nowrap for flex-wrap: the input + label-word (always short) stay together, and the descriptive text wraps to a second line if the row would otherwise overflow. Inline comment in the JSX explains the gotcha so a future dev doesn't reintroduce whitespace-nowrap. No change to spacing in the common case (text fits, layout identical).

title: #217 SUPERSEDED-by-#221 Log button + Cancel decision
description: SUPERSEDED-by-#221 (1.7.0-dev.63) — refined into #221: "bring back Log as the third inline button, but leave Cancel in the hamburger." The user's #221 request was the canonical ask; #217 was the broader earlier draft and is closed in favour of the narrower decision. Result: Queue rows now show three inline actions (Rerun · Clear · Log) plus the hamburger; Cancel stays under "Job" in the hamburger because cancellation is destructive and shouldn't be a one-click target on every in-progress row.

title: #216 Edit YAML moved out of Queue Job group into Device group
description: covered by #221's rework. Edit YAML moved out of the Queue-tab hamburger's "Job" group and into the shared DeviceMenuSection (rendered at the bottom of the Device list — Live Logs / Compile history / Restart / Copy API Key / Ping / Install to address / Edit YAML). The Devices-tab hamburger leaves the new optional onEdit prop unset because that tab already has an inline Edit button on every row, so DM2's user expectation is preserved on both surfaces.

title: #215 UpgradeModal reordered + decluttered
description: UpgradeModal reordered + decluttered. Sections now read top-to-bottom Action → Worker → Version (was Worker → Version → Action), so the verb that drives every other decision lands first. The ESPHome-version surface collapses to a two-radio choice — `Current (<defaultEsphomeVersion>)` and `Other` — matching the same shape Worker and Action use. Picking "Other" unfolds the existing search box + scrollable list + "Show betas" checkbox; picking "Current" hides them entirely, removing ~80 vertical pixels of clutter from the common case where the server-default version is fine. Existing selectedVersion state plumbing is unchanged (empty string still means "Latest / use default at compile time"); the new versionMode state seeds to 'other' when the rerun-seed or pinned-version pre-selects a non-default value, so a re-opened modal lands on the picker the user previously interacted with. All 187 mocked Playwright tests stay green against the rewritten layout.

title: #214 fixed cc1: posix_spawnp ENOENT compile failures on Mac Docker workers
description:
fixed cc1: posix_spawnp: No such file or directory compile failures on home-lab Mac-hosted Docker workers. Verified live by firing Compile All Online against hass-4 (65 online targets); pre-fix had 3 cc1 failures on macdaddy.localdomain's pio-slot-1, post-fix the worker self-heals and the retry succeeds.

Two complementary changes:

1. dev.59 — Clean Cache preserves the toolchain home. _clean_build_cache() now preserves anything starting with pio-slot- in addition to ESPHome venvs (#119). Pre-fix, every "Clean Cache" click wiped the worker's PLATFORMIO_CORE_DIR (~500 MB toolchain) and forced a 5–10 min re-download on the next compile — the failure mode where a parallel job races a half-extracted toolchain was a side-effect, not the steady-state bug. Inert macOS-only _strip_quarantine_xattr helper from #204 is removed (it was a no-op inside Linux Docker workers and a misleading red herring in the codebase).

2. dev.60 — worker self-heal. When a compile fails AND the captured log carries the cc1 / posix_spawnp signature (CMake's "Check for working C compiler … broken" + the literal "cc1: posix_spawnp: No such file or directory" line from gcc), the worker now: (a) dumps the toolchain layout to the log via _log_toolchain_state() for triage, (b) wipes pio_dir/packages/ via the new _wipe_broken_toolchain() so PlatformIO re-extracts on the next run, and (c) retries the compile in-process before submitting failed. The retry path applies to both the OTA esphome run and the download-only esphome compile flows. PlatformIO does NOT recover on its own — pre-fix, every job that landed on a worker with a broken toolchain failed identically forever; the operator had to ssh and rm -rf the toolchain manually. Now the first job to hit the breakage carries a 5–10 min re-download tax and every subsequent job lands on a fresh toolchain.

Both changes deploy via the existing in-place .py source-update path; no IMAGE_VERSION bump per PY-4. Five new tests in tests/test_client.py: test_clean_build_cache_preserves_esphome_venvs_and_pio_slots + test_clean_build_cache_removes_unprotected_dirs cover (1); test_wipe_broken_toolchain_removes_packages_dir + test_wipe_broken_toolchain_noop_when_packages_missing + test_wipe_broken_toolchain_swallows_rmtree_failure cover (2). Live verification: Compile All Online against hass-4 with macdaddy.localdomain online had pio-slot-1 broken pre-fix; post-dev.60 the same fleet should complete with zero cc1 failures (operator can re-confirm via the script at /tmp/compile-online-check.sh, which fires the run and greps every failed-job log for the cc1 signature).

title: #213 Ping result no longer overflows the modal
description: Ping result no longer overflows the modal. The <table> layout was squishing the muted label cells into ~1 character per line whenever a long RTT triple stretched the value column, and forcing a horizontal scrollbar on narrow viewports. Replaced the table with a <dl> grid (grid-cols-[max-content_minmax(0,1fr)] gap-x-3 gap-y-0.5); labels carry whitespace-nowrap so they always render on one line, the value column takes the remaining width and lets the RTT triple wrap if needed instead of growing. No information lost; existing e2e/ping-device.spec.ts (3 tests) stays green against the rewritten layout.

title: #212 archived devices with packages render friendly_name
description: archived devices that compose their esphome: block via packages: / <<: !include (e.g. athom-plug-3.yaml) once again render their friendly_name in the Archived view. Pre-fix scanner.get_archived_device_metadata only ran the raw permissive YAML loader, which treats !include / packages: as opaque scalars — the esphome.friendly_name lived in the included file and was never read. Fix: try _resolve_esphome_config first (which handles packages + includes the same way esphome compile does — already memoized by mtime, returns None gracefully on broken references) and fall back to the raw loader only when full resolution fails or leaves the friendly_name / area empty. New tests/test_scanner.py::test_archived_metadata_resolves_friendly_name_via_packages regression-pins the fix; the existing test_archived_metadata_unparseable_yaml_falls_back_to_empty test guarantees the raw fallback still kicks in for legitimately broken archives.

title: #211 Config-history Download chip from job-history binary
description: Config-history drawer rows now offer a Download chip when a successful compile at that commit's config_hash still has its firmware binary on disk. New JobHistoryDAO.latest_firmware_by_hash(target, hashes) returns {hash: {job_id, firmware_variants}} for the subset of commits that have a matching binary (newest job wins on duplicate hashes; binaries evicted by retention surface as firmware_variants=[] → row dropped). /ui/api/files/<file>/history enriches each entry with optional firmware_job_id + firmware_variants; HistoryPanel.CommitRow renders the existing icon-sized FirmwareDownloadMenu (.bin / .bin.gz × factory / ota) when those fields are present and lifts its open state to the panel (downloadMenuOpenHash keyed by hash) so SWR refreshes don't tear it down. Two new unit tests in tests/test_job_history.py cover the happy path (kept binary surfaces, evicted binary drops, failed jobs ignored, unknown hashes absent) and the empty-input short-circuit.

title: #210 only_eligible_worker reason firing on non-tag-targeted jobs
description: selection_reason="only_eligible_worker" ("Only eligible by tag" in the UI) was firing on jobs that weren't actually tag-targeted. Root cause in api.py claim handler: the busy-skip (if not other_free: continue) ran before the tag-eligibility check, so a fully-booked competitor — eligible by tag, just out of slots — was indistinguishable from a tag-disqualified one. Fix splits the count into eligible_other_count_any (tag-eligible regardless of busy state, drives the only_eligible_worker reason hint) and eligible_other_free_count (eligible AND free, drives the deferral logic). Now a fleet where every other worker is eligible-but-fully-booked falls through to first_available rather than misreporting as "Only eligible by tag". Regression test in tests/test_api.py::test_busy_other_workers_do_not_trigger_only_eligible_reason.

title: #209 Queue rows expose only Rerun + Clear inline; rest in hamburger
description: Queue rows now expose only Rerun + Clear inline; everything else (Cancel, View log, Edit YAML, Download firmware variants, plus the full Devices-tab Device-section actions: Live Logs, Compile history, Restart, Copy API Key, Ping device, Install to address) lives in a per-row hamburger menu. The Device section is rendered via a new shared DeviceMenuSection.tsx component that both the Devices-tab DeviceContextMenu and the Queue-tab QueueRowMenu consume — DRY by construction so the two surfaces can't drift. The Config-section actions (Pin/Rename/Duplicate/Config history/Rendered config/Commit/Archive/Delete) deliberately stay Devices-tab-only — Queue rows are compile attempts, not edit surfaces, and config-mutating actions belong where users land to make changes. Hamburger open state is lifted to the QueueTab parent (keyed by job id) so the 1 Hz SWR poll can't tear it down mid-click — same pattern #2 / #71 use. Existing e2e/compile-download.spec.ts, e2e/history-diff-since-compile.spec.ts, and e2e/modal-sizing.spec.ts updated to drive the new menu shape (open hamburger → click "View log" / Download variant items); 187 mocked e2e tests stay green.

title: #208 bulk Archive/Unarchive Selected items added
description: bulk "Archive Selected" / "Unarchive Selected" items added to the Devices Action menu. DeviceTableActions.tsx derives allSelectedActive (every checked row is non-archived) and allSelectedArchived (every checked row is archived) from the selected target objects; "Archive Selected" enables only when allSelectedActive is true, "Unarchive Selected" only when allSelectedArchived. Mixed selections, all-archived selections (for Archive), and all-active selections (for Unarchive) leave the matching item disabled with an explanatory tooltip — there's no single sensible action for a mixed set and the user shouldn't have to guess which subset would be touched. Both handlers fan out via Promise.all and surface one summary toast (per the project's "batch operations get one toast" rule). Wired through the existing deleteTarget(filename, archive=true) and restoreArchivedConfig(filename) API client functions — no new server endpoint, no schema change.

title: #207 archived devices show Archived status
description: archived devices no longer show "Checking…" indefinitely; the Status column renders "Archived" with a muted dot. useDeviceColumns.tsx short-circuits the status accessor with 'e-archived' (sorts past every live state) and the cell renderer with <StatusDot status="archived" /> before the activeJob / online / null / online-true / online-false fall-through. New 'archived' member on the Status type in StatusDot.tsx, with a muted text-[var(--text-muted)] colour so the row reads as inert at a glance. New .dot-archived class in theme.css (grey + 0.55 opacity, no animation — distinguishes archived from offline by being clearly de-emphasised rather than identical-coloured). The lastSeenEl date suffix is omitted on archived rows since the row is inert; the Tags-column archived chip already carries the relative archived-at timestamp.

title: #206 ping diagnostic always failed on HAOS
description: ping diagnostic always failed on HAOS instances. Root cause: HAOS ships kernel default net.ipv4.ping_group_range = 1 0 (an empty range — unprivileged datagram ICMP is disabled), so icmplib.async_ping(privileged=False) raised SocketPermissionError immediately; the addon container also lacked CAP_NET_RAW so the privileged=True raw-socket path wasn't available either. Two-part fix: (a) ha-addon/config.yaml now declares privileged: [NET_RAW] so the container can open raw ICMP sockets (AppArmor profile is already permissive on network/capability, no change needed), and (b) both ping helpers (/ui/api/targets/<f>/ping in ui_api.py and device_poller._ping_device) try unprivileged datagram ICMP first and fall back to the raw-socket path on SocketPermissionError. Standalone Docker deployments without NET_RAW keep working as long as the host's ping_group_range allows it (the unprivileged path); HAOS now works via the raw-socket fallback. New live-HA spec in e2e-hass-4/ping-device.spec.ts with two tests: one POSTs /ui/api/targets/cyd-office-info.yaml/ping and asserts the response shape is populated (target / address / packets_sent=10 / numeric RTTs / boolean is_alive — without asserting whether the device is reachable, since the device may be off when the smoke runs); the other walks through the UI (hamburger → Ping device → modal opens → result section appears) and asserts the modal does not render "Ping failed".

title: #205 PingDeviceModal rebuilt around shadcn slots
description: PingDeviceModal rebuilt around shadcn's structural slots so it matches the same layout treatment InstallToAddressModal got in #200 / BulkTagsEditDialog got in #17. Title alone in DialogHeader; the spinner / error / result table + RTT stats sit in one <div className="px-4 py-3 flex flex-col gap-2 text-sm text-[var(--text)]"> body block (was previously px-1 py-2 which didn't match anything else, and the result table's text-[var(--text-muted)] colour leaked onto the data cells); Copy / Run again / Close anchored in DialogFooter (Close wrapped in DialogClose so Esc / overlay-click stays consistent). Dropped the inline style={{ maxWidth: 480 }} since DialogContent already caps at 480px. Existing e2e/ping-device.spec.ts still passes against the rebuilt JSX.

title: #204 SUPERSEDED-by-#214 macOS Gatekeeper xattr stripping
description: SUPERSEDED-by-#214 (landed 1.7.0-dev.54 / reverted 1.7.0-dev.59) — initial fix added a _strip_quarantine_xattr() helper hypothesising macOS Gatekeeper was killing PlatformIO's downloaded Xtensa toolchain. The hypothesis is wrong: the affected workers run our published Linux Docker image, so sys.platform is not "darwin" inside the container and the helper never fires. The xattr code was inert in the actual reproduction environment. Removed in dev.59; real cause + fix tracked under #214.

title: #203 archived devices retain scanner-pulled attributes
description: archived devices now retain every attribute the scanner can pull from raw YAML, so the row keeps its tags / area / project / comment / pinned_version / schedule / network / chip / BLE-proxy after archive. The DM.1 archive merge in ui_api.get_targets was instantiating archived rows with every attribute hardcoded to None / False / "off"; new scanner.get_archived_device_metadata(config_dir, filename) re-reads <config_dir>/.archive/<filename> via the existing read_device_meta (comment-block path) + _load_raw_yaml + _fill_missing_metadata (raw YAML path), skipping the expensive _resolve_esphome_config because archived files may legitimately reference deleted secrets / packages. Devices-tab tagPool in DevicesTab.tsx now includes archived rows' tags only when showArchived is true (otherwise the pill bar would offer tags that filter no visible row). 4 new unit tests in tests/test_scanner.py cover the empty-shape fallback, tags+area+project preservation, schedule preservation, and graceful handling of unparseable YAML.

title: #202 CI test job ModuleNotFoundError after #197 landed
description: CI's test job had been red on every push since tests/test_git_clean_after_ops.py landed in dev.46 (#197) — ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests' at collection time. The file's `from tests.test_ui_api import _UiApp, _make_ui_app, _write_config` only resolves when tests/ is importable as a package (i.e., has an __init__.py), which it isn't — pytest's default `--import-mode=prepend` adds each test's directory to sys.path so siblings are importable without the prefix (every other cross-test import in the suite uses `from test_ui_api import …` accordingly). 1-line fix: drop the tests. prefix to match the rest of the suite. Discovered while running `gh run list` after the dev.52 push and seeing the red CI run had already been failing for 7 commits. No production code change.

title: #201 tag editing disabled for archived devices
description: tag editing is now disabled for archived devices on every surface that exposed it. Per-row Tags-cell button in useDeviceColumns.tsx becomes a read-only <span> for archived rows (matching the "—" empty-state instead of "+ tags" so the cell reads as locked, with a tooltip explaining "Unarchive first to edit"); the per-row hamburger never offered Edit Tags so it's already correct (it collapses to Unarchive + Permanently delete). Bulk "Edit Tags…" in DeviceTableActions filters its selection set to non-archived rows: when every selected row is archived the menu item disables with the tooltip "Tags can't be edited on archived devices"; mixed selections proceed against the active subset and the success toast appends "(N archived skipped)" so the user knows the archived rows weren't touched. The BulkTagsEditDialog's count + tag-aggregate (common/partial) reflects the editable subset only, so the dialog shows what we'll actually write rather than a misleading intersection that includes tags from rows we're about to skip. Same reasoning the per-row hamburger uses (DM.1 in #3): writes to YAMLs that live in .archive/ are ignored by the active fleet, so making them appear editable is dishonest UX.

title: #200 InstallToAddressModal rebuilt around shadcn slots
description:
InstallToAddressModal rebuilt around shadcn's structural slots so it matches the commit-message dialog's layout. Title alone in DialogHeader (with the --surface2 background it's meant to have); the body — description text, OTA-address Label+Input, the auto-resolved hint / invalid-address message, and the override-confirm warning — sits in one
block (was previously split across two siblings using px-1 pb-2 padding that didn't match anything else); Cancel + Install live in a DialogFooter (Cancel wrapped in DialogClose so Esc / overlay-click stays consistent with the rest of the surface). Dropped the inline style={{ maxWidth: 480 }} since DialogContent already caps at 480px by default, and dropped DialogDescription because the body's leading

carries the same text in the same style as the commit dialog. No behaviour change; the validation gate, single-step-when-unedited install, and two-step confirm-on-edited-address paths are byte-identical. Existing e2e/install-to-address.spec.ts (4 tests) all still green against the rebuilt JSX.

title: #199 firmware added to ha-addon/config.yaml backup_exclude
description: added firmware to ha-addon/config.yaml's backup_exclude: block alongside esphome-versions, with a code comment pointing at this bug. Same reasoning #45 used: firmware binaries are large, weakly-compressible (ESP .bin is already compressed → ~30% tar.gz ratio), and regenerable from the YAML that's already backed up via the HA core /config snapshot path. On hass-4 today the partial-addon backup "ESPHome Distributed Build Server" is 237 MB, ~100% of which is /data/firmware/ compressed. Job-history metadata (device, version, timestamp, log, success/fail, selection reason) stays in /data/job-history.db (1.7 MB) and continues to be backed up — the user keeps the record of every build, just not the artifact. Drops every ESPHome Fleet partial backup from ~237 MB → ~2 MB and shaves the same off every full HA snapshot. Pairs with #198 — once retention caps the on-disk footprint at "≤ 2 days of compiles," the backup-exclude is belt-and-suspenders that also covers users who set retention to 0 (unlimited) or who run a heavy multi-day batch right before a snapshot. Origin: user-reported backup-size investigation 2026-04-29 — /data/esphome-versions (14 GB) is already excluded, so firmware/ was the only remaining contributor.

title: #198 firmware artifact retention
description: firmware artifact retention. New firmware_retention_days Settings field (default 2, range 0..3650, 0 = unlimited) drives the new firmware_storage.enforce_retention helper. The existing firmware_budget_enforcer task is renamed to firmware_retention_enforcer (main.py) and now runs two passes per 30-min tick: (1) time-based — delete every .bin whose mtime is older than the configured retention window, with only live-queue jobs protected (history-protected binaries are deliberately NOT exempt — that's the whole point); (2) budget-based hard ceiling (the prior enforce_budget flow), where both live-queue + history are protected. The Settings drawer's "Disk management" section gains a matching "Firmware retention (days)" IntRow alongside firmware_cache_max_gb. Pair fix: JobHistoryDAO.evict_older_than now returns the evicted row IDs (was int) so the JH retention task in main.py can call delete_firmware(jid) on each evicted row — pre-fix the binary lingered on disk until the next add-on restart's reconcile_orphans sweep, and the JH retention WARNING reported "evicted N rows" while disk usage didn't actually drop. Tests: 6 new in tests/test_firmware_storage.py (enforce_retention evicts past-cutoff files / protects live-queue jobs / evicts history-protected binaries / no-op on days≤0 / no-op on missing dir / per-variant independence on the same job); 2 updated in tests/test_job_history.py for the new list[str] return shape; tests/test_settings.py::test_settings_as_dict_round_trips + tests/test_ui_api.py::test_get_settings_returns_defaults_on_fresh_boot + tests/test_api_contract.py updated for the new field. UX: History tab's per-row Download already handles evicted binaries gracefully (list_variants returns [] → no Download chip rendered) — no UI change needed; users who want a >2-day-old build hit Compile / Rerun, same as after a fresh restore. Pairs with #199.

title: #197 every UI-driven file op must leave git tree clean
description: every UI-driven file op must leave the git working tree clean. New tests/test_git_clean_after_ops.py drives 12 endpoint scenarios (save / create-staged + save / archive / permanent-delete / archive-restore / delete-from-archive / rename / pin / unpin / meta / schedule-set / schedule-clear / secrets-edit) plus a negative control, drains debounced commits, and asserts dirty_paths(config_dir) returns an empty set afterward. While building it, the archive-then-`git status` scenario surfaced a real dangling-files bug: archive_and_commit ran git mv correctly but its _staged_paths() filter used `git diff --cached --name-only`, which collapses a rename to just the destination — so the subsequent `git commit -- <pathspecs>` ran with only the dest path, committing the addition while leaving the staged deletion of the source dangling in the index. Fixed by switching _staged_paths() to --name-status and parsing the R100\tsrc\tdst form so both halves of every rename get included; archive / restore commits now land atomically. New CLAUDE.md invariant PY-11 documents the rule + points at the test file as the regression net for any future file-mutating endpoint. Origin: user-reported "we leave dangling files open in Git, examples: secrets, archive moves" — same bug class as #94.

title: #117-upgrade-changed Devices-tab Upgrade Changed item
description:
Devices-tab Upgrade dropdown gets an "Upgrade Changed" item between "Upgrade Outdated" and the separator. Filters via the existing hasDriftedConfig(t) helper — same source-of-truth the per-row "config changed" badge and Upgrade-button color use, so the menu picks exactly the set the user already sees marked. Distinct from "Upgrade Outdated" (firmware-version mismatch); this targets configuration drift (config_drifted_since_flash || config_modified fallback). Disabled when no targets carry drift. Modal title pluralises ("1 changed device" / "5 changed devices"). E2E: two new specs in bulk-upgrade.spec.ts cover the disabled-empty case and the open-with-drifted-only-count flow.

Note: this entry was numbered "117" (unhashed informal form) in the source file at the same time as the #117 hashed bug above. Imported here as "117-upgrade-changed" to disambiguate.

title: #116-services esphome_fleet.compile/validate accept tags filter
description:
esphome_fleet.compile and esphome_fleet.validate services now accept a tags list + optional tags_op matcher (any_of default; all_of / none_of available — same vocabulary the routing engine and per-job worker_tag_filter already use). Compile resolves the expression to every matching managed device's YAML filename via the coordinator's /ui/api/targets snapshot; validate enforces single-target match (multi-match raises tags_match_multiple, zero-match raises no_targets_match_tags). Resolution precedence: device picker > tags > targets. Archived rows are skipped. UI surface: new fields exposed in services.yaml so the action editor renders tags (object) + tags_op (select). Tests: _resolve_tags_to_targets unit tests cover each operator + archived-skip + token trim + empty-input edge cases; handler tests cover compile multi-match enqueue, validate single-match, multi-match rejection, zero-match rejection, and device-picker-wins precedence; schema tests pin the new optional fields + reject unknown ops.

Note: this entry was numbered "116" (unhashed informal form) in the source file at the same time as the #116 hashed bug; the file-author appears to have used the bare number as a separate item. Imported here as "116-services" to disambiguate from the hashed #116 bug above.

title: #113 Rendered-config modal stripped of ESPHome INFO/WARNING noise
description: Rendered-config modal showed the YAML buried between ESPHome's INFO / WARNING log lines. "Before" the YAML: INFO ESPHome 2026.4.3, INFO Reading configuration..., any "WARNING GPIO# is a strapping PIN..." from validation. "After" the YAML: a trailing "INFO Configuration is valid!" line. None of those belong in a "rendered config" view — users want the YAML and only the YAML. Root cause: ui_api.get_rendered_config ran esphome config <yaml> with stderr=asyncio.subprocess.STDOUT, merging the streams, then passed the combined captured bytes through to the modal as output. ESPHome's command_config (esphome/__main__.py:672) writes the rendered YAML to stdout via safe_print and writes its _LOGGER.info(...) chatter to stderr; the merge collapsed that separation. Fix: capture stdout and stderr separately. On success, output = stdout (clean YAML, including the existing \033[5m...\033[6m ANSI markers around password:/key:/psk:/ssid: values that the modal already renders). On failure, output = stderr + stdout because the validation error message lives in stderr and the actionable diagnostic context is what users want there. Cache key + secret-redaction logging contract unchanged. Verified live against cyd-office-info.yaml on hass-4 — pre-fix output started with five INFO/WARNING lines, post-fix output starts with substitutions: (the actual rendered YAML's first key).

title: #115 Upstream ESPHome bugs surfaced by #111 investigation
description:
Upstream ESPHome bugs surfaced (and worked around) by #111's investigation, tracked here so future readers know they're known-and-deferred-to-upstream:

1. git.clone_or_update (esphome/git.py:96) is not safe under concurrent invocation. Multiple processes computing the same <data_dir>/<domain>/<sha8>/ destination race with no file lock — losers see partial-state trees and surface as "Could not find components folder for source", "<file> does not exist in repository", or arbitrary AssertionError from incomplete merges. Our #111 fix (server-wide asyncio.Lock) avoids the race by serialising bundle subprocesses; the underlying ESPHome assumption is "single-process invocation," which their CLI honours but our parallel-claim server doesn't. Right answer at the upstream level is to add an fcntl.flock (or equivalent) inside clone_or_update. File as an esphome/esphome issue with our concrete repro; our fix doesn't depend on it landing.

2. esphome.core.config._is_target_platform does not catch AssertionError. It catches KeyError and ImportError, but get_component(name, True) opens with assert "." not in domain (esphome/loader.py:222). Any caller that passes a dotted name through this path crashes with a stack trace instead of a clean "Platform missing" error. We hit this in #111's race aftermath (a partial packages-pass left Kauf.PLF12 in the top-level config); the lock fix prevents the partial-merge state, but a future call site could trigger the same assertion. Right answer at the upstream level is to widen the except to include AssertionError, or to not assert at all — let _lookup_module return None for invalid module names. File upstream alongside (1).

3. The 2026.4.3 "Including a single package under packages: … is deprecated" warning is a false positive for the dict-of-strings form (packages: { Name: github://... }). Already fixed upstream by PR #15605 (8790dec13 — "Fix false deprecation warning and wrong error paths in nested packages") but not in 2026.4.3 itself. Auto-resolves on the next ESPHome point release that includes the fix; #112's logger silencing prevents the warning from polluting our captured stderr in the meantime so users never see it from us.

title: #114 covered by #220 expanded #214 self-heal
description: covered by #220's expanded #214 self-heal. The fresh repro arrived in a 21-failure home-lab batch on 2026-05-01 (AI-MacBook-Pro.localdomain + 3 other workers): the "esptool is not a package" signature shipped alongside "Missing framework-arduinoespressif32-libs package", "tool-esptoolpy does not appear to be a Python project", and /penv/bin/esptool: not found → Error 127 — every one of them a different facet of the same broken pio-slot-N/ tree, none of them caught by #214's narrow cc1-only matcher. The original "force-reinstall would risk other failure modes" disposition holds, but the right action for all of these symptoms is the same one #214 already implemented for cc1 — wipe the corrupted subtrees and let PIO re-bootstrap. #220 broadens both the signature pattern set and the wipe target (now includes penv/, where bin/esptool actually lives), so the next occurrence self-heals on retry without operator intervention.

title: #110 Routing-rule conflict detection in Upgrade modal
description: Routing-rule conflict detection in the Upgrade modal: when the user picks a Specific worker or a Tag expression whose chosen workers don't satisfy a global routing rule that fires for the affected target(s), the modal surfaces a warning panel listing the offending rule names. Clicking the now-relabelled "Upgrade & override rules" confirm button sends bypass_routing_rules: true to /ui/api/compile so the server enqueues the job anyway; the user's pin / tag-filter is still honoured because that's their explicit constraint, not the rule's. Implementation: new bypass_routing_rules: bool = False field on the server-side Job dataclass (round-tripped through to_dict / from_dict for queue persistence; tolerant load reads pre-#110 queue.json files as False). JobQueue.enqueue accepts the flag and stamps it onto new jobs (and forwards it onto follow-up coalesced jobs). routing_eligibility.re_evaluate_routing's check callback returns (True, None) early when the flag is set so a conflicting job never goes BLOCKED. routing_eligibility.build_claim_eligibility's per-worker predicate likewise returns True early when the flag is set, but still rejects workers that fail the user's worker_tag_filter (the user's other explicit constraint). /ui/api/compile accepts bypass_routing_rules: bool in the body and plumbs it through. Client side: compile() API gains an optional 6th bypassRoutingRules?: boolean arg; the UpgradeModal fetches active routing rules via SWR (only loaded while the modal is open), computes conflicts client-side using the existing utils/routing.evaluateClause mirror, and surfaces a warning panel + relabelled confirm button when a conflict exists. App.tsx materialises per-target tag lists from the affected Target.tags strings and passes them as affectedTargetTags so the modal can detect device-tag matches without a server round-trip. Tests: 3 new unit tests in tests/test_routing_eligibility.py cover bypass-clears-block, bypass-lets-ineligible-worker-claim (with the worker-tag-filter still enforced), and round-trip persistence; 4 new mocked Playwright specs in e2e/rerun-and-routing-override.spec.ts cover the rerun-opens-modal flow (#109) plus the conflict-warning + bypass-flag round-trip and the no-conflict-no-warning case (#110). The conflict detector handles both specific worker (compare worker tags directly) and tag expression (find any online worker that satisfies both the user's filter and the rule's worker_match) modes; the latter falls back to the live worker pool when the user's filter shape isn't a strict superset of the rule's clause. Per-device routing_extra from device YAML metadata is honoured server-side via the existing eligibility cache; client-side conflict detection focuses on global rules for now (the common case).

title: #109 Per-row Rerun opens UpgradeModal pre-populated
description: Per-row Rerun (Queue tab + LogModal) now opens the unified UpgradeModal pre-populated with the original job's worker / version / action / tag-filter so the user can tweak any field before re-submitting, instead of silently re-enqueuing through /ui/api/retry. Single-target callers go through the modal; bulk reruns (Rerun All Failed, Rerun Selected) keep the immediate /ui/api/retry path because there's no single set of params to seed a modal with. Implementation: new optional seed: { pinnedClientId, workerTagFilter, esphomeVersion, action } prop on UpgradeModal initialises workerMode (specific → tag → any in priority order), selectedWorker, tagFilterOp/tagFilterTags, selectedVersion, and the action radio (download-now vs upgrade-now). App.tsx's upgradeModalTarget state shape gains an optional seed field; new rerunSingleJobViaModal(job: Job) helper builds the seed from the job's existing fields and opens the modal targeting [job.target]. handleRetryJobs short-circuits to the modal path when ids.length is 1. The standard compile() flow handles stale-job cleanup automatically (its enqueue call already removes terminal jobs for the same target), so the original failed/successful row gets pruned the same way /ui/api/retry would.

title: #108 Every redo this job affordance now reads as Rerun
description: Every "redo this job" affordance now reads as Rerun in the UI. The historical split (Rerun for a successful job vs. Retry for a failed/timed-out job) collapsed onto the single verb because the distinction never carried meaning beyond colour — re-running a failed job and re-running a successful job both produce a fresh compile against the current YAML. Per-row buttons in the Queue table + the LogModal share a <Button> whose variant (success / warn) still flips with the source-job state, so the colour signals "this row failed" without changing the verb. The bulk dropdown trigger and items move from "Retry" / "Retry All Failed" / "Retry Selected" to "Rerun" / "Rerun All Failed" / "Rerun Selected"; toast text shifts from "Retrying X" to "Rerunning X". Internal symbol names (retryJobs, handleRetryJobs, RetryResponse, /ui/api/retry) stay put because none are user-visible. The standalone EsphomeInstallBanner's "Retry the failed install" button stays as-is — that one really is a retry of a transient failure, not a rerun of finished work. The "OTA Retry" job-state badge for ota_only jobs likewise stays — it names a specific kind of pending work, not a user action. Updated e2e/queue-extras.spec.ts "failed job uses Retry" → "failed job also uses Rerun" so the regression guard for the new label moves with the change.

title: #107 bulk-upgrade items now route through UpgradeModal
description: All four bulk-upgrade dropdown items (Upgrade All / Upgrade All Online / Upgrade Outdated / Upgrade Selected) now route through the same UpgradeModal the per-row Upgrade button uses, so the user picks worker / ESPHome version / action (Upgrade Now, Download, Schedule Recurring, Schedule Once) up-front and the chosen options apply uniformly across the affected device set. Implementation: App.tsx's upgradeModalTarget state shape changed from a single target: string to targets: string[]; new handleOpenUpgradeModalMany(targets, displayName, defaultMode) callback opens the modal for a multi-device set; existing handleOpenUpgradeModal wraps a single target into a 1-element array. handleUpgradeConfirm now POSTs the entire target list in one compile() call so the server-side "Queued N device(s)" toast reads correctly. Schedule saves (onSaveSchedule / onSaveOnce) fan out across the target list via Promise.all of setTargetSchedule / setTargetScheduleOnce. DevicesTab gets a new onUpgradeMany prop; the four menu items materialise their target lists (all, online not false, needs_update, selectedTargets) and call into it with a human-readable displayName ("all 12 devices", "5 outdated devices", etc.) that surfaces in the modal title and the success toast. Disabled-state on each item now reflects whether its set is non-empty (Upgrade Outdated stays disabled when nothing's outdated; Upgrade Selected stays disabled when no rows are checked). The UpgradeModal itself needed no changes — when t is undefined (multi-target case) the existing pinnedVersion/currentSchedule/currentOnce props go undefined and the modal's shouldUpdatePin and hasExistingSchedule derivations flip false, suppressing the pin warning + the "Remove existing schedule" button without any new flag. Pin update (params.updatePin) is gated on targets.length being 1 in handleUpgradeConfirm so a multi-device upgrade never touches a per-device pin. Dead handleCompile + onCompile plumbing removed from App.tsx and DevicesTab — every entry point now goes through the modal.

title: #106 Commit all uncommitted opens commit-message dialog
description: "Commit all uncommitted" now opens a shadcn Dialog with an optional commit-message input before fanning out the per-target commits. Same shape as the per-row Commit changes… dialog in App.tsx (label, placeholder, default-fallback copy) so the two surfaces feel identical. Empty input → undefined → server falls back to the default "Manually committed from UI" marker for every file in the batch; non-empty → that subject is applied to every commit so the resulting git history reads as one logical operation. Dialog open + entered-message state owned in DeviceTableActions; the existing fan-out loop in handleCommitAll is unchanged except for the message arg passed through to commitFile. Cancel during in-flight commit is suppressed (onOpenChange is gated on commitAllBusy) so a half-fanned-out batch can't be orphaned.

title: #105 missing rebuilt UI bundle for #103
description: the dev.30 commit for #103 only included the source change in DeviceTableActions.tsx; I forgot the matching rebuilt UI bundle under ha-addon/server/static/assets/. The Dockerfile only `COPY server/ .`'s the pre-built bundle (no `npm run build` inside the image), so every deploy after dev.30 carried the old bundle that doesn't have the new menu item. Verified by greping the deployed bundle on hass-4 — it has Commit changes…, Edit Tags, Schedule Selected, Remove Schedule from Selected, but Commit all uncommitted is missing. Fix: rebuild the UI (npm run build) and commit the regenerated static/ files together with the source change going forward. Filing this as a doc note: dev-plans/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md (and the per-turn rhythm) should be loud about "rebuild + commit the static bundle whenever ha-addon/ui/src changes" — it's currently silent.

title: #104 Workers tab Tags column moved to position 2
description: Workers tab Tags column moved to position 2 (right after Hostname), mirroring the Devices tab's #16 layout. Tags drive routing rules + bulk operations; the eye sees them faster as the second column than buried six positions in. Implementation: reorder HEADER_ORDER in WorkersTab.tsx and the matching <td> cells in both the single-slot and multi-slot row paths (TanStack supplies the headers via the array; cells are hand-written, so the two have to stay in lockstep). Column-defs themselves untouched — the order array is what drives the visible column sequence. All 150 mocked Playwright specs still green; existing routing-rules.spec.ts "Tags for build-server-1" selector is position-agnostic so no spec churn.

title: #103 Commit all uncommitted entry on Devices tab
description: "Commit all uncommitted" entry added to the Devices tab Actions dropdown. When the user has edited YAMLs outside the addon (CLI, file share, another editor) the working tree fills with dirty files; the per-row hamburger commits one at a time which is tedious for a fleet-wide cleanup. The new item filters targets for has_uncommitted_changes, fans out via Promise.all(commitFile(t)) (mirroring the pattern already used by SettingsDrawer's auto-commit-onboarding flow), and surfaces a single summary toast — "Committed N", "Committed N, M failed", or "No files committed". Disabled with an explanatory tooltip when there's nothing dirty (No uncommitted YAML changes in the config directory); hidden entirely when versioning is off. The label includes a live count (N) so the user sees how much is queued before clicking. SWR onRefresh runs on completion so the per-row "config changed" indicators clear in the same tick.

title: #102 _device_compile_epoch parser updated for ISO-with-tz format
description: _device_compile_epoch was hard-coded to the older "%b %d %Y, %H:%M:%S" shape ("Mar 29 2026, 17:00:00") that the dev.18 fix-comment for bug #13 referenced, but aioesphomeapi actually reports the build time as "2026-04-23 06:13:56 -0700" (ISO with timezone offset). Result: the parser silently returned None for every device, the SQLite-history fallback path never produced a ~time value, and any target without server-side compile history showed a bare "—" in the Devices tab "Last compiled" column even when the device was clearly running compiled firmware. Fix: extracted the parser to a module-level _parse_device_compile_epoch() in ui_api.py, try the modern format first then the older comma-form for back-compat. Tz-aware parses produce a UTC-correct epoch; tz-naive parses fall back to server-local time as before. Updated the Device.compilation_time docstring to use a current-format example. 3 new unit tests in tests/test_ui_api.py: modern format with offset round-trips correctly, old format still parses, unknown shapes / None / empty / ISO-with-Z (unsupported by %z in 3.11+) all return None without raising.

title: #101 Queue tab Routing column folded into Worker selection
description: Queue tab Routing column folded into the existing "Worker selection" column (the two answer related questions: what the user asked for vs why this worker won — reading them as one cell tells the routing story without bouncing eyes between columns). Top line shows the routing intent — small "📌 specific worker" when pinned_client_id is set, `{op-label} <TagChips>` (e.g. `any of [linux]`) when worker_tag_filter is set, nothing for the unconstrained default so default rows don't grow a placeholder line. Bottom line keeps the existing selection-reason label (Pinned / Only eligible / Least busy / Fastest / First to poll) with its hover tooltip. Cell falls back to "—" when neither line has content. The standalone Routing column is gone; the Worker column (where the job landed) is unchanged. Implementation: single column-def edit in ha-addon/ui/src/components/QueueTab.tsx; formatSelectionReason + TagChips already in scope. All 150 mocked Playwright specs still green.

title: #97 Upgrade modal worker-selection radio with three modes
description: was open as "29" in the local sequence: Upgrade modal worker-selection radio with three modes — any worker (default; routing rules + scheduler decide), specific worker (legacy single-worker pin), or tag expression (all_of/any_of/none_of clause against the fleet's worker tag pool). Backend: Job.worker_tag_filter: dict | None (shape {op, tags} matching a routing-rule clause), serialised through to_dict/from_dict, plumbed through JobQueue.enqueue, and honoured by routing_eligibility.build_claim_eligibility — the per-worker predicate that drives the bug #95 claim_next check rejects a worker whose tags don't satisfy the filter (malformed clauses fall through as "no constraint" so a typo doesn't strand a job). API: /ui/api/compile accepts worker_tag_filter in the request body, validates op + non-empty cleaned tag list before passing through. UI: RoutingClauseOp type re-exported on Job; compileTargets API client takes a 5th workerTagFilter arg; App.handleUpgradeConfirm plumbs it through and surfaces the chosen filter in the success toast ((workers any of [linux, macos])). UpgradeModal's Worker section is now a 3-way radio with conditional sub-controls: the Specific dropdown only appears when "Specific" is selected, the op-dropdown + chip-input clause builder only when "Tag expression" is selected. Confirm button disables until "Specific" has a worker / "Tag expression" has at least one tag. The chip-input pulls autocomplete from the worker-tag pool only (matches RoutingRuleBuilder's worker side). 3 new unit tests in tests/test_routing_eligibility.py (filter honoured, every op behaves correctly, malformed filter is permissive). All 146 mocked Playwright specs pass against the rebuilt UI.

title: #100 Queue tab Routing column
description: Queue tab Routing column mirrors the Upgrade modal's worker-selection radio so the user can read each job's routing intent off the table. dev.26 reworded the column's leading comment to dodge the UI-3 explicit any type invariant's regex (a `: any` pair inside English prose tripped the same grep that bug #26 already documented in dev.16). The existing Worker column shows where the job landed (assigned hostname); the new column inserted right after it shows what the user asked for: "Any worker" (muted italic) when neither pin nor tag-filter is set, "<Pin> Specific worker" when pinned_client_id is set (Pin icon matches the Worker column's existing affordance + the modal's pinned semantics), or `{op-label} <TagChips>` when worker_tag_filter is set — same TagChips component and djb2-hash colors as the Devices/Workers/RoutingRules surfaces, so a linux chip on a Queue row visually matches a linux chip on the Workers tab. Op renders lowercase ("all of" / "any of" / "none of") to match the casing used across the Routing-rule builder UI. Tooltip on each variant explains the scheduling implication. Sort key is the canonical 0-any < 1-tag:op:tags < 2-specific so alphanumeric sorting groups by intent type. Implementation: single column-def in ha-addon/ui/src/components/QueueTab.tsx (+TagChips import); no API or schema changes — the data already arrives via Job.pinned_client_id / Job.worker_tag_filter (#97). All 146 mocked Playwright specs still green.

title: #98 routing-rule stalemate from per-worker eligibility check
description: routing-rule stalemate when the per-worker eligibility check (#95) leaves a higher-perf worker ineligible. Reproduced live against hass-4 after #95 deployed: a garage-door-big.yaml (ratgdo-tagged) compile sat PENDING for ~5+ min with no claim. Root cause: api.py's should_defer rule 2 ("defer to a worker with higher effective score") didn't know about routing eligibility. OPTIPLEX-7 (windows, the only eligible worker) polled, saw macdaddy/AI-MacBook-Pro had higher perf scores, and deferred. Those macos workers polled, hit the per-worker eligibility check from #95, returned None. Stalemate — no progress until something changes the worker pool. Fix: pre-compute the set of PENDING jobs I am eligible for, then in the deferral loop only count an "other" worker as a real alternative when they're eligible for at least one of those same jobs. A faster-but-ineligible worker is no longer treated as a deferral candidate. New regression test in tests/test_routing_eligibility.py::test_eligibility_isolates_stalemate_when_only_one_worker_qualifies pins the predicate-side contract; the api.py wiring is exercised by the matrix's live ratgdo round-trip on hass-4.

title: #99 worker-selection reason misleading when routing narrows eligibility
description: Queue tab's worker-selection reason was misleading when routing rules narrowed eligibility to a single worker. Symptom (live on hass-4 after #98 deployed): garage-door jobs claimed by OPTIPLEX-7 (the only windows-tagged worker) carried selection_reason="first_available" ("First to poll"), making it look like OPTIPLEX-7 won a race against equally-eligible peers. Reality: the routing rule disqualified every other worker, so there was no race — OPTIPLEX-7 was the only eligible candidate. Fix: track eligible_other_candidate_count separately from other_candidate_count in api.py's deferral loop (the eligibility filter from #98 already provides the signal). When other_candidate_count > 0 but eligible_other_candidate_count is 0 and we have eligible PENDING work, the reason becomes the new "only_eligible_worker" ("Only eligible by tag") — distinct from "only_online_worker" ("Only worker online") because other workers were online; the rule, not their absence, narrowed the field. UI mapping in utils/selectionReason.ts adds the new pill + tooltip; type-doc strings in types/index.ts and job_queue.py updated. The "first_available" label still applies when multiple workers are equally eligible — which now means equally-eligible-after-routing.

title: #95 routing rules silently bypassed for devices with eligible workers
description: routing rules silently bypassed for any device whose rule has at least one eligible worker online. Symptom (reported live against hass-4): rule "Garage Doors on Windows" set device_match: any_of [ratgdo] + worker_match: all_of [windows], but garage-door-big.yaml ran on macdaddy (macos) and garage-door-small.yaml ran on local-worker (debian). Root cause: JobQueue.claim_next only filtered out BLOCKED jobs and respected pinning — it never checked per-worker eligibility for PENDING jobs. The fleet-wide re_evaluate_routing sweep correctly held a job PENDING when any online worker satisfied the rule (OPTIPLEX-7 in this case), but every other worker was free to claim. Fix: new is_eligible callback parameter on claim_next (ha-addon/server/job_queue.py); new build_claim_eligibility(app, worker_tags) builder in ha-addon/server/routing_eligibility.py returns a closure with a per-call target-meta cache; api.py's claim_next handler builds the predicate from the registry's per-worker tag list and passes it through. Pinned jobs bypass the predicate (pinning is the user's explicit override; mismatched pin + rule is a user-visible conflict, not silent stranding). 4 new unit tests in tests/test_block_state.py (predicate accepts/rejects/pin-bypass/back-compat-when-omitted); 4 new tests in tests/test_routing_eligibility.py (no-rules short-circuit, ineligible vs eligible worker, per-device routing_extra honoured, target-meta cache hit). Bug number jumped to #95 to keep the bug index globally monotonic per CLAUDE.md — the local 1.7.0 sequence (1–26 + the open #27 placeholder) and the archive's pre-existing #28–#94 had collided otherwise.

title: #96 ConnectWorker autocomplete pool narrowed to worker tags only
description: was open as "27" in the local sequence: ConnectWorker dialog autocomplete pool was the union of device + worker tags, which surfaced device tags (kitchen, cosy, ratgdo) as suggestions when configuring a worker. Narrowed to worker tags only — same source the routing-rule builder's worker side reads. Single inline change in App.tsx's tagSuggestions prop. (Renumbered to #96 to keep the bug index globally monotonic per CLAUDE.md.)

title: #26 green CI on develop after dev.15
description: green CI on develop after dev.15. Two failures: (a) tests/test_ui_api.py::test_get_settings_returns_defaults_on_fresh_boot was missing date_format: "auto" in the asserted dict — the field landed in dev.10 (#5) but the test expectation wasn't updated, so dev.15 went red. (b) the UI-3 explicit any type invariant was matching English prose in WorkersTab.tsx comments ("OR-logic: any selected tag matches", "no worker has any tag yet") because the regex `:[[:space:]]*any` happens inside human sentences too. Rephrased both comments — the regex itself is intentionally simple and cheap; tightening it to skip line-comments adds risk without much win when the offending phrasing is easy to dodge.

title: #25 chip-input + autocomplete dropdown for ConnectWorker Tags field
description: chip-input + autocomplete dropdown for the ConnectWorker Tags field. Extracted the chip-input from TagsEditDialog into a reusable TagChipInput component (components/ui/tag-chip-input.tsx) — same UX (Enter/comma to commit, Backspace on empty input drops the last chip, autocomplete dropdown filtered by substring) — and dropped it into ConnectWorkerModal's Tags field. The form still serialises to the comma-joined string WORKER_TAGS expects, so the docker invocation output is unchanged. App.tsx computes the fleet-wide tag pool inline (union of every device + worker's tags) and passes it via the new tagSuggestions prop. TagsEditDialog rebased onto the shared component so future chip-input changes apply to both consumers in one place.

title: #24 palette doubled from 12 to 25 colors
description: palette doubled from 12 to 25 colors. The dev.10 (#21) Tailwind-600 set used 12 hues at the same lightness — adjacent slots like red/orange or violet/fuchsia/pink read as "the same color" at chip size when the hash collided. Now: all 17 Tailwind 600 hues (red / orange / amber / yellow / lime / green / emerald / teal / cyan / sky / blue / indigo / violet / purple / fuchsia / pink / rose) cover the full spectrum, plus 7 Tailwind 800-stop darker variants of widely-spaced hues add a second perceptual band so two tags hashing to the same hue can still differ by lightness, plus slate-600 as the neutral. White text reads AA-contrast on every entry. Same djb2 hash so existing tag→color mappings stay stable for hashes that still land in the first 12 slots; the new slots are extra capacity for fleets with more tags.

title: #23 two new optional Devices-tab columns: ESP + BLE proxy
description: two new optional Devices-tab columns: ESP (chip family parsed from the resolved YAML — "ESP32", "ESP32-S3", "ESP8266", "RP2040", "Host") and BLE proxy (off / passive / active from the bluetooth_proxy: block presence + active: field). Both default off so existing layouts don't shift; users opt in via the column-picker. Slot in the table order between AP and Schedule, and the picker order is updated to match (bug #19's invariant). Server side: scanner._extract_metadata reads esp32: (with variant: for S2/S3/C3/C6/H2 — rendered as "ESP32-S3" not "ESP32S3" to match Espressif product naming), esp8266:, rp2040:, host:. Bluetooth proxy uses key-presence (not truthiness — bare bluetooth_proxy: parses as None but still enables the component, same web_server pattern from #74). 6 new tests in tests/test_scanner.py cover variant rendering, all chip families, and the three BLE states.

title: #22 update_target_meta per-key commit-message action
description: update_target_meta derives a per-key commit-message action from the request body. Single-key bodies fan out to specific subjects via the new `meta <key>` / `meta <key> cleared` action keys (e.g. "Updated device tags", "Cleared device tags", "Pinned ESPHome version", "Updated routing rules"); multi-key bodies still fall back to the generic "Updated device metadata". _default_subject also degrades uncurated `meta <key>` actions to "Updated <key>" so a future metadata field landing without a curated entry still reads cleanly. Existing dedicated endpoints (/pin, /schedule) keep their already-specific messages. New test_default_subject_specific_meta_keys covers curated + uncurated + cleared paths.

title: #21 chip color palette redesigned for visual distinctness
description:
chip color palette redesigned for visual distinctness. The dev.6 → dev.9 hue list ([0, 30, 60, 95, 130, 160, 190, 215, 240, 270, 300, 335] at HSL 65/45) had clusters that read as "the same color" at chip size — 130+160 both green, 215+240 both blue, etc. Replaced with a curated 12-color palette derived from Tailwind's 600 stops (red / orange / amber / lime / green / teal / sky / blue / violet / fuchsia / pink / slate) — each color has a perceptibly different hue + saturation so a row of 4 tags reads as 4 distinct colors at a glance. Plus a same-hue darker border for a faint "frame" that helps separate adjacent same-color chips when the hash maps two tags to the same slot.

Also in 1.7.0-dev.10 (no separate bug entries — diagnosed from dev.9 matrix logs):
- e2e-hass-4/incremental-build.spec.ts SPEEDUP_THRESHOLD bumped 1.50 → 2.00 after hass-4 hit ratio=1.70 in dev.9 (cause: other compiles running on local-worker during the test compile #2 — host-load variance, not a cache regression). Docstring updated with the host-by-host ratio table.

title: #20 autocomplete dropdown anchored to chip-input
description: autocomplete dropdown anchored to the chip-input, replacing the dev.7 "Suggestions" section. Pops below the input as the user types, lists matching fleet-wide tags as colored chips, click-to-add. Only renders when input has non-empty text — dropping the focus-only mode keeps the dialog from hovering chips over the Apply button right after Enter clears the input. onMouseDown rather than onClick on the dropdown items so the input's onBlur (which closes the dropdown immediately) doesn't race with the click handler. Same change applied to the bulk dialog. Both TagsEditDialog and BulkTagsEditDialog.

title: #19 column-picker order invariant
description: column-picker order invariant. Picker order now matches the table column order exactly (Tags column moved from position 13 in the picker to position 1, mirroring the table's "Device → Tags → Status …" layout). New e2e/column-picker-order.spec.ts opens the Devices tab, scrapes the table header text in render order, opens the column-picker, scrapes the checked picker labels, and asserts the visible-picker labels appear as a subsequence of the headers in the same order — fails CI if a future column move drifts the two out of sync.

title: #18 Areas column rendering JSON
description: Areas column rendering JSON. Root cause: ESPHome's newer schema accepts area: {name: "...", id: "..."} (dict) in addition to the original area: "..." (string), and scanner._extract_metadata was calling str() on the dict — str({...}) produces Python's repr ("{'name': 'Living Room', 'id': '...'}") which surfaced verbatim in the cell. Now both _extract_metadata and _fill_missing_metadata normalise to the dict's name field (falling back to id). Two new regression tests in tests/test_scanner.py: test_metadata_extracts_area_from_dict_form and test_metadata_extracts_area_from_dict_form_id_fallback.

title: #17 modal layout rebuilt around shadcn structural slots
description: modal layout rebuilt around shadcn's structural slots. Title alone in DialogHeader (the header is flex justify-between and was previously fighting the DialogDescription for horizontal space — squashed both); description + chip-input + suggestions live in a padded body section; Cancel + Save anchored in DialogFooter (with the dark --surface2 background that shadcn renders for footers). Floating × close button suppressed (showCloseButton={false}) since Cancel is the explicit dismiss path. New regression spec in e2e/tags-modal-layout.spec.ts (3 tests: structural slots present, footer below chip-input, dialog fits a 480×600 viewport with footer reachable).

title: #16 Tags column moved to position 2
description: Tags column moved to position 2 (right after Device on Devices tab; right after Hostname on Workers tab). Chip text bumped 10px → 12px, padding 0/1.5px → 0.5/2px, cell min-width: 120px so a single chip doesn't squeeze into illegibility. The "+ tags" empty-state placeholder also bumped to 12px so it reads consistently with the populated state.

title: #15 dropped leading Lucide Tag icon from TagChip
description: dropped the leading Lucide Tag icon from TagChip. Chip color + text already say "this is a tag"; the icon ate horizontal space in narrow table cells.

title: #14 background refresh wiping in-progress edits
description: background refresh wiping in-progress edits. The TagsEditDialog's seed-from-initial useEffect previously listed initial in its dep array, which fired on every parent SWR poll (1Hz) because the parent passes a fresh array reference each render even when values are unchanged — overwriting the user's typed text mid-edit.
