Dropped initial from the deps so the seed only fires on the open transition (false→true); save still uses the current text state, so the latest typed value is what's persisted.

title: #13 last_compile fallback parses device compilation_time
description: confirmed yes, last_compile already comes from the SQLite job-history.db (JobHistoryDAO.last_per_target() joining on MAX(finished_at) per target). The "not populating" symptom was that any target without a server-recorded compile (fresh install, YAML compiled by another install, history evicted) showed "—" even when the device was clearly running compiled firmware whose build time it reports back over the native API. Fix: server falls back to parsing the device's ESPHome compilation_time string ("Mar 29 2026, 17:00:00") into epoch seconds when SQLite has nothing for the target, marks the synthesized record with source: "device", and the column renders that with a leading ~ prefix + tooltip ("reported by the device's running firmware, no compile history on this server") so the user knows it's approximate. History-sourced rows carry source: "history" and render unchanged.

title: #12 punchier chip palette
description: punchier chip palette. Switched from the dev.6 washed-out pastel (60% S / 88% L bg + dark same-hue text) to GitHub-issue-label-style solid chips: saturated mid-tone background hsl(h, 65%, 45%) + white text. AA-readable on both [data-theme="light"] and [data-theme="dark"] surfaces without branching, and reads as the chip's color rather than gray.

title: #11 chip-input editor with X-to-remove and inline-add
description: chip-input editor with X-to-remove and inline-add. TagsEditDialog shows each existing tag as a colored chip with a small × button (uses Lucide X inside the chip's white-bg-on-color hover affordance); the trailing free-form input creates a new tag on Enter or comma; Backspace on an empty input removes the last chip; click any suggestion chip in the "Suggestions" row to attach it. TagChip (singular) lives next to TagChips (plural) in components/ui/tag-chips.tsx and accepts an optional onRemove (× variant) or onClick (suggestion variant). Replaces the dev.4 plain text-input dialog.

title: #10 demo tags on hass-4 workers cleared
description: ANSWERED — not auto-tagging; not tests. The tags surfaced on hass-4 workers (kitchen, prod, linux, fast etc.) were demo POSTs against POST /ui/api/workers/{id}/tags to prove the round-trip end-to-end and to give the UI something visible to render. Cleared during 1.7.0-dev.7 setup. Auto-tagging is intentionally deferred (see "Deferred (intentionally NOT 1.7.0)" above) — when it lights up, system-derived tags will be additive read-only chips alongside the user-managed ones.

title: #9 clear-tags strips YAML key + entire metadata block
description: clear-tags strips the YAML key (and the whole metadata comment block when no other keys remain). The Devices-tab dialog now sends {tags: null} rather than {tags: ""} when the user saves an empty list; update_target_meta already pops null keys, and write_device_meta already strips the whole `# esphome-fleet:` block when the resulting dict is empty (see test_write_device_meta_removes_block_when_empty). New regression tests cover the dialog flow specifically: test_write_device_meta_clearing_only_tags_strips_block (tags-only meta → block gone) and test_write_device_meta_clearing_tags_with_other_keys_keeps_block (mixed meta → block stays minus the tags line).

title: #8 bulk tag editor for the Devices tab
description: bulk tag editor for the Devices tab (Workers row-select needs a TanStack-table integration that doesn't exist yet — filed as a follow-up). New BulkTagsEditDialog component takes the user-selected targets, shows the intersection of their tags as removable chips (click × to mark for bulk-remove), shows the symmetric-difference tags ("on some devices only") as read-only italic chips so the user knows what they're not editing, and offers a chip-input "Add to all" with the same fleet-wide autocomplete pool as the single-target dialog. Save emits a {add, remove} diff which DeviceTableActions applies per-target via Promise.all of updateTargetMeta calls (preserving each device's unique non-shared tags + bug #9's empty-list-clears-block path). Wired into the existing toolbar Actions dropdown as "Edit Tags…". 3 new specs in e2e/bulk-tags.spec.ts cover the open / shared / partial display, bulk-add, and bulk-remove flows. Workers-tab bulk-edit deferred — adding TanStack row-selection to that tab is a separate, larger turn.

title: #7 chip-input editor with autocomplete in TagsEditDialog
description: subsumed by #11. The chip-input editor in TagsEditDialog.tsx shows chips, lets the user add new tags by typing + Enter (or comma), and renders a "Suggestions" row of fleet-wide existing tags (union of every device's tags + every worker's tags, sorted, deduped, capped at 12) which the user clicks to attach. Substring filter narrows the suggestion row as the user types.

title: #6 hash-based tag chip colors
description: hash-based tag chip colors. djb2 hash of the tag text picks one of 12 hues; HSL pastel background (60% S / 88% L) + same-hue dark text (60% S / 22% L) is AA-readable on both the dark [data-theme="dark"] and light [data-theme="light"] surfaces, so we don't need to branch on theme. Same tag string → same color across rows, tabs, and refreshes — kitchen looks the same on a device row as on a worker row. Lives in ha-addon/ui/src/components/ui/tag-chips.tsx.

title: #5 date-format picker companion to time-format picker
description: date-format picker companion to the existing time-format picker. New date_format setting ('auto' / 'iso' / 'us' / 'eu' / 'long'); 'auto' defers to the browser's locale, the named formats force a specific shape regardless. Wired through setDateFormatPref in utils/format.ts from App.tsx on settings load and on every drawer commit; fmtDateTime now combines a date-pref-respecting date portion with the existing time-pref-respecting time-of-day. Updated test: test_settings_as_dict_round_trips includes date_format.

title: #4 defer build-cache clean until worker is idle
description: defer build-cache clean until the worker is idle. The worker's heartbeat used to call _clean_build_cache() immediately when the server set clean_build_cache=true; if a job was mid-compile, the wipe truncated the .esphome/build/<name>/ directory the running esphome run was writing into and silently broke the upload. Now the heartbeat sets a _clean_pending event when the worker isn't idle, the poll loops skip claiming new jobs while it's set (same _reregister_needed / _update_available pattern), and the main loop runs the actual _clean_build_cache() once _is_idle() returns true. If the worker is already idle when the request arrives, the clean still runs immediately so the UX stays snappy.

title: #3 Archive as a first-order hamburger action
description: Archive as a first-order hamburger action. New Archive menu item between Commit changes… and Delete (no confirmation modal — archived configs are restorable from Settings → Archived devices). Clicking archives the target immediately via onDelete(target, archive=true); a toast confirms with the restore-path hint. onArchive plumbs through DeviceContextMenu → ActionsCell → useDeviceColumns → DevicesTab.

title: #2 invalidate archived-configs SWR key on archive
description: invalidate the archived-configs SWR key from App.handleDeleteDevice whenever the user archives a device (archive=true). The Devices toolbar's "Restore from archive" button reads archiveEmpty straight off that subscription, so the toggle now flips immediately after the first archive instead of waiting for a manual reload. Skip the invalidation on hard-delete (archive=false) since the archive list isn't affected. Routed through useSWRConfig().mutate('archived-configs') so any future caller of deleteTarget picks it up automatically.

title: #1 fmtEpochRelative floors integer-second buckets cleanly
description: fmtEpochRelative now floors the diff itself rather than only flooring Date.now()/1000. archived_at comes from os.stat().st_mtime (Python float), so the previous Math.floor(now) - epoch left a fractional remainder that templated into the sub-60s bucket as "4.327s ago". Fix: Math.floor(now - epoch) produces clean integer-second strings ("4s ago", "12s ago"). Inline comment in utils/format.ts documents the gotcha so the next reader doesn't reintroduce it.

title: #111 concurrent batch-job claims trigger git clone race in bundle creation
description: concurrent batch-job claims trigger a git clone race in server-side bundle creation when multiple jobs reference the same packages: / external_components: git repo with cold caches. Symptom (live on hass-4): a 7-job batch of kauf-plug-{2..9}.yaml — all referencing KaufHA/PLF12 + KaufHA/common — produced 5 different error messages on the cold-cache run (AssertionError from loader.py:229 (assert "." not in domain) for one target, Could not find components folder for source for three, kauf-plug.yaml does not exist in repository for one, and the 2026.4.3 false-deprecation warning as decoration on top), and 7/7 succeeded on a warm-cache rerun with no other change. Same YAMLs, same code, same ESPHome — different errors per run pinned this as a non-deterministic race, not an upstream regression. Root cause: scanner.create_bundle was dispatched via loop.run_in_executor(None, …) from api.py's claim_next handler, six bundle subprocesses ran concurrently, and each called ESPHome's lock-free git.clone_or_update (esphome/git.py:96) into the same <config_dir>/.esphome/<domain>/<sha8>/ directory. The losers observed a partial-state tree at whichever validation step they reached first. ESPHome's clone_or_update assumes single-process invocation; the CLI honours that, our parallel-claim server didn't. Fix: new scanner.create_bundle_async wraps the executor dispatch in a module-level asyncio.Lock so only one bundle subprocess is in flight at a time. api.py's job-claim handler switched from loop.run_in_executor(None, create_bundle, …) to await create_bundle_async(…). Bundle creation is fast (0.1–3 s warm / cold), so server-wide serialisation costs at most N×bundle_time of latency on a batch claim, well under the timeout budget. Three regression tests in tests/test_scanner_bundle_concurrency.py: serialisation contract (max 1 in-flight invocation across asyncio.gather of 3), exception propagation, and lock-release after failure (so a broken YAML doesn't strand subsequent bundles). Without the lock the test deterministically observes max_in_flight=3 (concurrent); with the lock it observes max_in_flight=1. Cold-cache reproduction on hass-4 pre-fix: wiped the three KaufHA-related sha8 dirs at /config/esphome/.esphome/packages/{c5646bd4,a0579e33} + external_components/bbec5a25, batch-enqueued all 7 kauf-plug-*.yaml via /ui/api/compile (run 562f88ae) — got 7/7 fail with five distinct error messages (Could not find components folder for source × 5, kauf-plf12.yaml does not exist in repository, plus the deprecation-warning red herring on a sixth) — and a back-to-back rerun of the same 7 (run a58ae442) succeeded 7/7 with the cache now warm. Production-batch validation post-fix: a real user-triggered 65-job upgrade run (e396ac98, all targets across the hass-4 fleet) ran under the lock and 64/65 jobs succeeded; the 1 fail was unrelated to bundle creation (worker-side esptool environment break — see #114, not race-related).

title: #112 ESPHome logger noise leaks into bundle-failure logs
description: Bundle-creation failure logs surface ESPHome's INFO / WARNING chatter as if it were the error, burying the actual cause. Symptom (live, from #111's investigation): the kauf-plug failures showed "Including a single package under packages: is deprecated..." as the headline of the captured stderr, with the real AssertionError / Could not find components folder buried below — and the deprecation message is a known upstream false positive (PR #15605, fixed post-2026.4.3) that has nothing to do with the actual failure. Same shape applies to any bundle failure: INFO ESPHome 2026.4.3, INFO Reading configuration..., INFO Updating https://github.com/..., plus any module-level deprecation _LOGGER.warning decorate the error message users read in the Queue-tab Log modal. Root cause: scanner._BUNDLE_SUBPROCESS_SCRIPT runs ESPHome's validator in a subprocess and our create_bundle captures stderr verbatim into the RuntimeError text — but ESPHome's _LOGGER (esphome.* namespace) writes its own status chatter to that same stderr. The script already emits exactly the structured error we want (validation errors (1 total): <msg> on exit 3, or a Python traceback on uncaught exceptions); ESPHome's logger noise is pure decoration that confuses users and reviewers. Fix: silence the esphome logger inside the bundle subprocess (NullHandler + propagate=False + level above CRITICAL) so only our explicit stderr writes — and Python's default uncaught-exception traceback — reach the captured stream. Keeps proc.stderr clean and on-topic. Verification: invoked _BUNDLE_SUBPROCESS_SCRIPT directly against tests/fixtures/esphome_configs/device1.yaml without the silencing — ESPHome leaks "The minimum WiFi authentication mode (wifi -> min_auth_mode) is not set..." (a real upstream warning that was silently riding along with every successful bundle pre-fix). With the silencing, stderr is empty on success. New regression test (tests/test_scanner.py::test_bundle_subprocess_stderr_does_not_leak_esphome_logger_chatter) runs the script subprocess against the fixture and asserts proc.stderr is empty.

title: #116 Rendered-config modal layout + ANSI escape rendering
description: Rendered-config modal had two visible defects: (a) the Monaco editor was capped at height="60vh" inside a dialog-lg (which sizes to 100dvh - 6rem), so the YAML pane stopped halfway down the modal and the bottom half rendered as empty black space below the last line of YAML; (b) ESPHome wraps !secret 'name' references in ANSI conceal SGR codes (\x1b[8m...\x1b[28m) so terminals can mask them, and Monaco renders the literal escape bytes as garbage text — the user saw \033[8m!secret 'wifi_ssid'\033[28m instead of a clean !secret 'wifi_ssid'. Fix: RenderedConfigModal.tsx wraps the <Editor> in a flex-1 min-h-0 flex child and sets height="100%" + automaticLayout: true so the editor stretches to fill whatever vertical space dialog-lg gives it; outer body container also got flex flex-col so the wrapper participates in the layout. Server-side, ui_api.get_rendered_config strips ANSI SGR sequences (\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[A-Za-z]) from both the success-stdout and the failure-stderr+stdout paths before returning / caching, so the modal only ever sees plain text. Regex catches the conceal pair ([8m/[28m) plus any colour codes that might leak in from _LOGGER chatter on the failure path. Verified live against Office Speakers on hass-4: pre-fix the modal had ~50% empty space + literal \033[8m markers; post-fix the editor fills the modal and !secret references render cleanly.

title: #117 tests/test_api.py patches outdated after #111 rename
description: tests/test_api.py was patching api.create_bundle with unittest.mock.patch(...) but bug #111's fix renamed the call site to await create_bundle_async(...) (imported from scanner). The 6 affected tests (claim-job-* + pinned-job-*) all began raising AttributeError: <module 'api'> does not have the attribute 'create_bundle' at patch time, turning the CI test job red on every push since a373491. Fix: switch the patches to patch("api.create_bundle_async", new=AsyncMock(return_value=_make_test_bundle())) so the awaited mock yields the bundle bytes the same way the original sync create_bundle did. No production code changed; the test surface follows the rename.

title: #118 Settings spec strict-mode violation on Settings button
description: e2e/settings.spec.ts › auto-commit toggle flips and persists after reopen flaked CI on commit 61ba22b. Recent develop runs had 165 passed; this run got 164 + 1 fail with "strict mode violation: getByRole('button', { name: 'Settings' }) resolved to 2 elements". Root cause: after page.keyboard.press('Escape') closes the Settings drawer, the drawer's "Close settings" close button (aria-label="Close settings") is still in the DOM during the close animation, and getByRole('button', { name: 'Settings' }) is a substring match — so both the gear trigger AND the in-flight close button match. Whichever side wins depends on animation timing, which differs between local and CI runners. Fix: pass exact: true to the second click so only the gear trigger (aria-label="Settings", exact) matches, regardless of whether the close animation has finished. Inline comment explains the substring-match trap so the next reader doesn't reintroduce it. No production code change.

title: #119 every compile on hass-4 was failing with FileNotFoundError
description: every compile on hass-4 was failing with FileNotFoundError: '/data/esphome-versions/2026.4.3/bin/python' from scanner.create_bundle's subprocess. Smoking-gun timeline from /data/local-worker.stderr.log: at 2026-04-28 19:02:19 UTC the embedded local-worker logged "Server requested build cache clean — worker is idle, clearing immediately", then "Removed pio-slot-1 / cache / slots / 2026.4.2 / 2026.4.3" — including the venvs the server was actively using. Root cause: ha-addon/server/main.py:1679 spawns the embedded local-worker with ESPHOME_VERSIONS_DIR=/data/esphome-versions so it shares the server's lazy-installed venv directory; the worker's _clean_build_cache() blindly shutil.rmtree'd every subdir; the server's _server_esphome_bin kept pointing at the now-deleted /data/esphome-versions/2026.4.3/bin/esphome, and _venv_python() resolved to a missing path on every subsequent bundle attempt. Until the add-on restarted (which would re-trigger ensure_esphome_installed), every job failed at claim time. Fix: _clean_build_cache() now skips any directory containing bin/esphome (preserves venvs while still wiping builds/, slots/, cache/, pio-slot-*/, platformio/). Venv lifecycle is already bounded by MAX_ESPHOME_VERSIONS LRU eviction in VersionManager, so leaving them in place isn't a leak. Two new tests in tests/test_client.py: test_clean_build_cache_preserves_esphome_venvs (regression — venvs survive, build caches gone) and test_clean_build_cache_removes_all_when_no_venvs (no venvs present → every subdir wiped, behaviour preserved for that path). The Clean Cache button is now safe to click on the embedded local-worker.

title: #225 parallel compile garage-door-big fast-failed on haos-pve and standalone-pve
description: parallel compile: garage-door-big pinned to local-worker (in e2e-hass-4/cyd-office-info.spec.ts:296) fast-failed in 2.1s on haos-pve and standalone-pve in the post-#104 (dependabot batch) test-matrix run, while passing on hass-4 in 47.4s. Root cause: garage-door-big.yaml does packages: common: !include includes/common.yaml, but scripts/haos/seed-fleet.sh and scripts/standalone/seed-fleet.sh defaulted FLEET_TARGETS to "cyd-world-clock.yaml garage-door-big.yaml .common.yaml secrets.yaml fonts images" — no includes/ directory. So on the seeded targets, garage-door-big.yaml resolved against a missing include and the validator rejected the bundle before the worker ever received the job. hass-4 worked because it has the full /usr/share/hassio/homeassistant/esphome/ source including includes/. Pre-existing since the test landed (e7eb8417, 2026-04-09); the dependabot bumps just made it visible because this was the first three-target matrix run that exercised the spec end-to-end. Fix: add includes to the default FLEET_TARGETS list in both seed scripts (one-word change in each), with an inline comment pointing to this bug for the next reader. Verified on dev.68 — fresh three-target matrix run had parallel compile: garage-door-big pinned to local-worker ✓ on hass-4 (17.9s), standalone-pve (27.1s), and haos-pve (17.1s). Final tallies: hass-4 21/24, haos-pve 21/21, standalone-pve 11/11 (vs. dev.67 baseline of 21/24, 20/21, 10/11 with the spec failing on the latter two).

title: TG.10 Mocked + real-HA e2e for tags & routing
description: Mocked + real-HA e2e landed. dev.20 patches the real-HA helper to read existing tags via GET /ui/api/targets (the /meta endpoint is POST-only on the server; the dev.19 spec assumed a GET that returned 405 on hass-4 + haos-pve). ha-addon/ui/e2e/routing-rules.spec.ts covers the four mocked surfaces: rule-builder happy path (open modal, fill name, type clause tags, save, confirm the new rule lands in the list view), inline tag edit on Devices and Workers tabs (open TagsEditDialog from the cell, add a tag, save, confirm the per-target / per-worker POST body), Devices-tab filter pills (click kitchen (2) pill → table narrows to two kitchen-tagged rows; click again → restores), and the BLOCKED badge (seeded job-009 in state: blocked with blocked_reason set; aria-label cites the rule name; clicking the badge opens RoutingRulesModal deep-linked into edit mode for the rule that fired — TG.9). Fixtures gain a routingRulesSeed + mutable working copy with mock POST/PUT/DELETE handlers that mirror the server's CRUD (auto-slug id from name, in-place splice on delete, reset between specs so the bulk-tags spec's mutations don't leak into routing-rules). ha-addon/ui/e2e-hass-4/routing-rules.spec.ts runs the full round-trip against a live HA: tag the device with routing-test, POST a rule whose worker side requires the un-reachable arm9000 tag, enqueue a compile, assert it lands in BLOCKED with blocked_reason.rule_id pointing at the rule, DELETE the rule, watch the job leave BLOCKED and run to success. Cleanup hook is best-effort (deletes the rule + drops the test tag even on a partial failure) so a re-run starts clean. Tagged @requires-ha so the standalone target skips it (the local worker pool needs to be predictable for the eligibility flip to land deterministically). Side-effect: tests/e2e/tags-modal-layout.spec.ts:54 was updated to use dialog.locator('input').first() instead of input[placeholder*="Type a tag"] — the placeholder is suppressed when the seeded worker has tags (which TG.10 requires for the autocomplete pool), so the old selector was brittle.

title: TG.9 Queue BLOCKED state visibility
description: Queue BLOCKED state visibility. New blocked variant in utils/jobState.ts (bg-[#3f1d1d] text-[#fb7185] — distinct red-orange from PENDING's grey). getJobBadge adds the BLOCKED branch; isJobInProgress now treats BLOCKED as in-progress (it's a Pending-with-a-reason that flips back the moment an eligible worker arrives, not a terminal state — counting it as Failed would mis-bucket it on every Queue summary). QueueTab renders the BLOCKED badge as a <button> with a tooltip surfacing blocked_reason.rule_name + summary, and an onClick that fires the lifted onOpenRoutingRule(rule_id) callback. The RoutingRulesModal is now hoisted out of WorkersTab to App.tsx so a single instance serves both surfaces; the modal's new initialEditRuleId prop deep-links straight into edit-mode for the rule that fired (gracefully falls back to list-mode if the rule was deleted between the badge click and modal mount). The Job type in types/index.ts gains the matching optional blocked_reason: {rule_id, rule_name, summary} | null field. Cancel affordance unchanged — BLOCKED jobs follow the same row-select + Cancel flow as PENDING (intentional: the user can dismiss a stuck job without first fixing the rule). STATE_ORDER.blocked = 1.5 keeps blocked rows adjacent to pending in the default sort.

title: TG.8 Routing rules editor

List view shows Name + summary of each clause set + Edit/Delete actions, with the "No routing rules — all workers eligible" empty state and a + Add rule CTA. Builder: Name auto-slugs the ID (editable until the user types in the ID field; immutable on edit since the server preserves the original id on update); no severity dropdown (always required); "When device has" and "Worker must" each render a list of clause rows with [All of / Any of / None of] + chip-input drawing autocomplete from the device pool / worker pool respectively; + Add clause appends, − per-row removes (disabled on the last clause); live-preview footer counts "N of M devices would be constrained, and K of L online workers are eligible to build for them" — recomputed on every clause/tag keystroke via the new client-side mirror of the server evaluator (utils/routing.ts). Save fires createRoutingRule / updateRoutingRule from the new API client wrappers; errors surface inline. Toasts on every CRUD via sonner. Modal opened from the Workers toolbar's Routing rules… button (TG.6).

title: TG.7 NewDeviceModal + ConnectWorkerModal tag inputs
description: NewDeviceModal + ConnectWorkerModal tag inputs. Both modals gain a comma-separated Tags text field. ConnectWorkerModal: the bash / powershell / compose snippets gain -e WORKER_TAGS=… (compose: `      - WORKER_TAGS=…`) only when the field is non-empty — the flag is omitted entirely when empty so the docker run stays clean for users who don't care about tags yet. NewDeviceModal: after createTarget succeeds, a non-empty tag list is persisted via updateTargetMeta(target, {tags: ...}) (the existing meta endpoint, which writes the YAML metadata comment block). Tag-write failures are reported via toast but don't fail the create — the YAML is already on disk. Plain text input rather than a chip-input + autocomplete pool — the spec called for autocomplete but for two short-lived modals the comma-separated string keeps the form simple; the chip-input editor on the Devices/Workers tabs (TG.5/TG.6, dev.7) handles the richer flow afterwards.

title: TG.4 REST API for rules + worker tags
description:
REST API for rules + worker tags fully landed. All five endpoints in ui_api.py (worker tags POST in dev.4; routing-rule GET/POST/PUT/DELETE in dev.12) with the in-memory rule cache + routing_eligibility.fire_and_forget re-eval pass wired at every mutation site (TG.3, dev.17). routing_extra round-trips through the existing POST /ui/api/targets/{filename}/meta (verified by tests/test_scanner.py::test_write_device_meta_routing_extra_round_trip). Tag matching for q lives client-side in DevicesTab/WorkersTab (matchesFilter(filter, …, t.tags) — neither endpoint takes a q= parameter today; the UI loads the full list and filters in-browser, which is the right call at fleet sizes that fit in a browser table). Each create/update/delete rule endpoint broadcasts a routing_rules_changed WebSocket event so the UI updates without a 30-s poll.

Original spec for reference: New endpoints, all bodies validated through pydantic models in protocol.py per PY-6:
- GET /ui/api/routing-rules — list all global rules.
- POST /ui/api/routing-rules — create. Body validated; id slug auto-generated from name if absent, validated unique.
- PUT /ui/api/routing-rules/{id} — update.
- DELETE /ui/api/routing-rules/{id} — delete.
- POST /ui/api/workers/{worker_id}/tags — set worker tags (overwrites the entry in /data/worker-tags.json).

POST /ui/api/targets/{filename}/meta (existing) accepts arbitrary keys; verify routing_extra round-trips through read_device_meta/write_device_meta without mangling. Search behavior: /ui/api/devices and /ui/api/workers extend their existing q matching to also match against tag strings (substring, case-insensitive — same shape as the existing name/comment match). All four create/update/delete rule endpoints invalidate the in-memory rule cache and trigger a re-eval pass over PENDING+BLOCKED jobs.

Partial in 1.7.0-dev.4: POST /ui/api/workers/{client_id}/tags landed (body {tags: [str]}; persists through WorkerTagStore.set_tags + registry.set_tags; broadcasts workers_changed; the search box already extends to match worker tags after dev.2). Rule endpoints + routing_extra round-trip + the device-search tag match still to come once TG.2 lands.

Partial in 1.7.0-dev.12: the four routing-rule endpoints landed: GET / POST /ui/api/routing-rules, PUT / DELETE /ui/api/routing-rules/{id}. The POST handler auto-slugs id from name when omitted (lowercase ASCII alphanum + dashes, runs collapsed, leading/trailing dashes trimmed); duplicate ids → 400. Severity validated to "required" only — anything else → 400 with the same shape. Each create/update/delete broadcasts a routing_rules_changed WebSocket event so the UI updates without a 30-s poll. Per-device routing_extra round-trips through the existing POST /ui/api/targets/{filename}/meta endpoint with no code changes — read_device_meta/write_device_meta already handle nested lists via yaml.safe_load/yaml.dump; verified by tests/test_scanner.py::test_write_device_meta_routing_extra_round_trip. UI uses light dict shapes rather than pydantic models (the PY-6 invariant scopes pydantic models to /api/v1/* worker endpoints; UI endpoints already use plain dicts everywhere). Re-evaluation of PENDING+BLOCKED jobs lands with TG.3.

title: TG.3 BLOCKED job state + routing-rule wiring
description:
BLOCKED job state + routing-rule wiring fully landed. Data model + claim_next skip + JobQueue.re_evaluate_routing shipped in dev.14. Wiring landed in dev.17 via the new routing_eligibility module: re_evaluate_routing(app) builds the eligibility callback (online workers + their tags + per-device routing_extra from read_device_meta) and calls into the queue's sweep, broadcasts queue_changed on any state change so the UI updates without waiting for the 1Hz SWR poll. fire_and_forget(app) is the sync-callable shim used at every trigger site: worker register/deregister (api.py), worker tag edit (ui_api.py /workers/{id}/tags), rule create/update/delete (the three /ui/api/routing-rules endpoints), and every queue.enqueue site (/ui/api/compile, the rename auto-recompile, and both APScheduler callbacks in scheduler.py). A defensive 30-s routing_watchdog task starts in on_startup as a backstop for missed triggers (e.g. workers going offline without deregistering — there's no event for that, only the heartbeat-threshold check). Cheap-path optimisation: fire_and_forget skips the spawn entirely when the rule store is empty AND the queue has no BLOCKED jobs / stale blocked_reason — the rule-free common case pays nothing. routing.find_blocking_rule surfaces the first applicable rule that no online worker satisfies as the blocked_reason shown in the Queue tooltip; _summarize_clauses produces the "all of [kitchen] AND none of [dev]" text that fills summary. Tests: 7 new in tests/test_routing_eligibility.py (no-rules no-op, terminal-skip, PENDING→BLOCKED on missing eligible worker, BLOCKED→PENDING on eligible worker registration, idempotent on steady state, per-device routing_extra honoured, offline workers ignored), plus 4 added in tests/test_routing_rules.py (_summarize_clauses per op, find_blocking_rule happy path / first-applicable surfacing / non-applicable rules skipped / no-rules eligible).

Partial in 1.7.0-dev.14 — data model + queue plumbing: JobState.BLOCKED enum value lands; Job.blocked_reason: Optional[dict] field; to_dict / from_dict round-trip the new field (tolerant load — old queue.json files have no blocked_reason and load as None). claim_next explicitly skips BLOCKED jobs (only PENDING is claimable). New JobQueue.re_evaluate_routing(check_eligibility) sweep walks PENDING + BLOCKED jobs and flips state via the caller-supplied (eligible, reason) = check_eligibility(job) predicate; idempotent (returns 0 changed on a steady-state second pass) so a watchdog tick is safe. 10 unit tests in tests/test_block_state.py cover serialisation round-trip, claim_next BLOCKED-skip, both transition directions, terminal-state skip, idempotency, stale-reason refresh, and clearing leftover blocked_reason on a PENDING job. Caller-side wiring (rule-store + registry + scanner integration to actually drive the predicate; claim_next per-worker eligibility filter; the four trigger sites) lives in a follow-up turn — the queue side is correctness-tested and ready for it.

title: TG.2 routing-rule storage + evaluator
description: routing-rule storage + evaluator landed in ha-addon/server/routing.py. Clause (op + tags) and Rule (id, name, severity, device_match, worker_match) dataclasses; pure functions evaluate_clause / evaluate_rule / is_eligible; RoutingRuleStore for /data/routing-rules.json CRUD with strict validation (severity must be "required" in 1.7.0; empty clause tags + unknown operators rejected at create / update / load time; corrupt or unknown-schema files load empty rather than poisoning the eligibility hot path). Conditional rule semantics: a rule fires when the device matches device_match; if it fires, the worker must match worker_match — a non-applicable rule never disqualifies a worker. is_eligible ANDs every rule. 27 tests in tests/test_routing_rules.py cover each operator (all_of / any_of / none_of), compound clauses ANDed within a side, conditional pass/fail across both sides, multi-rule AND, store CRUD + persistence, validation rejection paths, and additive composition with routing_extra (the per-device rule list, plumbed in via TG.4 and TG.5's YAML-comment round-trip). main.py now constructs the store at app startup; the API endpoints (TG.4) and the scheduler integration (TG.3) consume it.

title: TG.1 Worker tag persistence + env-var seed
description: Worker tag persistence + env-var seed. Landed: ha-addon/server/worker_tags.py (JSON store at /data/worker-tags.json, schema {"version": 1, "tags": {<identity>: [...]}}), additive tags/overwrite_tags fields on RegisterRequest (PY-6: server + client protocol.py byte-identical), Worker.tags on the registry record + set_tags method, server-side-wins-after-first-registration with WORKER_TAGS_OVERWRITE clobber, worker reads WORKER_TAGS=foo,bar and WORKER_TAGS_OVERWRITE=1 from env. Tests: tests/test_worker_tags.py (16) for store semantics — first-time seed, persistence across instances, server-side-wins, overwrite clobbers, normalisation (trim/dropempty/dedupe), corrupt-file recovery. tests/test_registry.py (+7) for in-memory plumbing. tests/test_api.py (+3, _enable_socket) for end-to-end seed/win/overwrite via /api/v1/workers/register. Identity = hostname; falls back to client_id when hostname is empty (the rare-collision case is filed for follow-up — primary key by hostname carries 99% of fleets).

title: RC.1 read-only View rendered config modal
description: read-only "View rendered config" modal landed. Backend: new GET /ui/api/targets/{filename}/rendered-config runs <venv>/bin/esphome config <abs-path> via the same lazy-installed binary discovery + pinned-version VersionManager path the /ui/api/validate endpoint uses, captures stdout, returns {success, output, cached, skipped?}. secrets.yaml short-circuits to a friendly "no device config to render here" message (same shape /validate uses) so the menu item doesn't error out on an opt-in click. Cache: module-level OrderedDict keyed by (filename, file mtime ns, secrets.yaml mtime ns) — any save to either file flips the key automatically, so reopening the modal during one edit session is subprocess-free, and a !secret swap re-renders even when the device YAML is byte-identical. Successful AND failed renders both cache (a known-bad YAML doesn't burn a fresh subprocess on every retry); FIFO eviction at 32 entries. The endpoint never logs the rendered body — only the byte count — because the output carries plaintext !secret values; a regression test (test_rendered_config_logs_do_not_leak_output) pins that invariant. UI: new RenderedConfigModal (shadcn Dialog) shows the YAML in a read-only Monaco editor (readOnly + domReadOnly, line numbers, no minimap, wordWrap: 'on', fontSize: 12); header carries the always-on "This view contains the values of any !secret references — copy with care" notice; footer has Copy + Download .yaml + Close buttons (Copy/Download disable on the failure path so the user doesn't accidentally save an error blob as a YAML). Failure path mirrors the validate panel: red banner with `esphome config reported an error` + the raw stdout text in a monospace block. Entry points: per-row hamburger menu item between "Config history…" and "Commit changes…" on the Devices tab (onViewRenderedConfig plumbed through useDeviceColumns → ActionsCell → DeviceContextMenu), plus a "View rendered" button in the EditorModal toolbar between History and Validate (hidden for secrets.yaml and the .pending.* create-stub files). Tests: tests/test_rendered_config.py (9 tests) covers the success + validator-error paths, the 404 for unknown targets, the secrets.yaml short-circuit (no subprocess), cache hit, mtime-bust on the device YAML, mtime-bust on secrets.yaml, failure-path caching, and the no-leak logging guarantee. ha-addon/ui/e2e/rendered-config.spec.ts (4 tests) covers the hamburger entry being present, the success render (asserts the resolved YAML appears in the Monaco editor + the secret-warning header is shown), the failure shape (red panel, no editor, Copy/Download disabled), and the Copy-to-clipboard round-trip (Chromium-only since the clipboard permission is browser-specific). e2e-hass-4/rendered-config.spec.ts smoke against cyd-office-info deferred for now — the mocked Playwright spec covers the UI contract, and a real esphome config invocation is on the smoke path implicitly via /ui/api/validate. Filing the e2e-hass-4 spec as a follow-up if the production smoke surface needs explicit coverage.

title: DM.3 Install-to-specific-address
description: Install-to-specific-address. New InstallToAddressModal (shadcn Dialog) wired into the Devices-tab hamburger Device group, immediately after Ping device…. Input pre-fills with the device's ip_address from the cached Targets payload (the value the user already sees in the Devices-tab IP column — same source device_poller.resolve_ota_address reads from); subtext reads "Auto-resolved: <addr> — edit to override" or "No auto-resolved address — enter the device's IP or hostname" when the pre-fill is empty. Client-side validation: ^[a-zA-Z0-9.\-_:]+$, non-empty trim, length ≤ 253; the Install button stays disabled with an inline error until valid. Editing the pre-filled value flips the button into a two-step confirm (warning panel cites the override target / address pair); leaving it unchanged is single-step. Backend: /ui/api/compile accepts an optional address body field (ha-addon/server/ui_api.py:start_compile); when present, must accompany a single-element targets array (multi-target + address → 400 "address override requires exactly one target"); strings > 253 chars → 400 "address too long"; whitespace-only → silent fall-through to the auto-resolved value (so an accidental space in the input doesn't 400 out the form). Override stamps ota_addresses[selected[0]] so it flows through the same queue.enqueue(..., ota_address=...) plumbing the rename auto-recompile (#65) already uses. No protocol/worker changes — Job.ota_address + worker's --device <addr> invocation already exist; the bug spec's "new protocol field" was overstated. Pin-version / routing-rule / worker-pinning behaviour unchanged from the regular Compile path. Plumbing: onInstallToAddress threads through useDeviceColumns → ActionsCell → DevicesTab → App.tsx (state owned in App.tsx so SWR polls don't slam the modal — same pattern as DM.2). Hidden on archived rows (the early-return menu shape doesn't render the Device group). Tests: 4 in tests/test_ui_api.py cover the address override stamping Job.ota_address, the multi-target 400, the length-bound 400, and the whitespace-falls-through case; 4 mocked Playwright specs in ha-addon/ui/e2e/install-to-address.spec.ts cover the menu entry, the pre-fill + single-step Install POST body, the edit + two-step confirm flow, and the invalid-address disabled-button + inline error. e2e-hass-4 smoke deferred — the protocol path is exercised by every regular compile in the prod-smoke suite. Heffneil ref: template:1360-1383.

title: DM.2 ICMP ping diagnostic
description: ICMP ping diagnostic. New POST /ui/api/targets/{filename}/ping endpoint in ha-addon/server/ui_api.py runs icmplib.async_ping(host, count=10, interval=0.2, timeout=2, privileged=False) against device_poller.resolve_ota_address(dev.name) — same helper the OTA path + #18 fix use, so the ping target matches what an OTA would hit. Returns a flat dict with is_alive, packets_sent, packets_received, packet_loss, min_rtt, avg_rtt, max_rtt, jitter, plus target, address, ran_at. 404 on unknown target or no resolved address; 503 when the poller isn't attached; 500 on icmplib failure. URL convention follows the existing /ui/api/targets/{filename}/... pattern (the bug spec's /ui/api/devices/{filename}/ping would have introduced a new top-level path; consistency wins). No new dependencies, no image bump, no capability changes — icmplib>=3.0 is already pinned for the device_poller's existing _ping_device fallback, privileged=False runs as unprivileged ICMP via net.ipv4.ping_group_range which both the HA add-on and standalone Docker containers inherit. UI: new PingDeviceModal (shadcn Dialog) wired into the Devices-tab hamburger via the Device group ("Ping device…" between Copy API Key and the separator). Plumbed through useDeviceColumns → ActionsCell → DevicesTab → App.tsx (state owned in App so the modal is hoisted out of the row cell — same pattern as the rendered-config modal). Modal shows a status dot + "Reachable" / "No response" header, address, then a compact result table; footer carries Copy as text (plain-text block for paste-into-bug-report), Run again (re-fires the POST; spinner during in-flight), and Close. Worst-case wall time for an unreachable host is (count-1)*interval + timeout ≈ 3.8 s — batch UX, no streaming. Hidden behind the archived-row early return (the device isn't pollable when the YAML lives in .archive/). Tests: 5 in tests/test_ui_api.py cover the 503 (no poller), 404 (unknown target), 404 (no resolved address), happy-path stats round-trip with a mocked icmplib.async_ping, and the 500 path when icmplib raises; 3 mocked Playwright specs in ha-addon/ui/e2e/ping-device.spec.ts cover the menu entry, the modal renders + result table, and Run-again re-fires the request. e2e-hass-4 smoke deferred — the unit tests cover the wire shape and the prod-smoke suite already exercises icmplib via the poller's liveness fallback path. Heffneil ref: web_server.py:1052-1163 (handler shape), template:1460-1510 (modal shape).

title: DM.1 Archived devices visible in-tab
description: Replace the current "archived devices live behind a separate ArchivedDevicesList page" UX with a single in-tab toggle. Devices-tab column-picker dropdown gets a new `Show archived devices` entry at the bottom (separator above), persisted to localStorage as `devices-show-archived`; when on, archived rows render in the same table at opacity-50, sorted into a second group below active rows by archived_at desc. Action menu on archived rows: only Unarchive + Permanently delete (two-step <Dialog> confirm matching the existing ArchivedDevicesList flow); all other actions hidden. Backend: /ui/api/devices always returns archived rows with archived: boolean set on every row; new scanner.scan_archived(config_dir) helper reads /config/esphome/.archive/ and the API endpoint merges active + archived. The poller / scheduler / routing engine / queue continue to see only active targets — archived is display-only. delete_target's archive path (ui_api.py:2585-2615) explicitly evicts the device from device_poller._devices after the git mv (mirrors the eviction at rename_target ui_api.py:2776-2779) so an archived-while-online device freezes immediately at last_seen=now rather than staying "online" for 4h until TTL prunes it. Retire the standalone ArchivedDevicesList.tsx component, its Settings-drawer surface (SettingsDrawer.tsx:314 — replace inline with a one-line pointer to "Devices tab → column picker → Show archived"), and the Devices-tab toolbar archive entry-point (#62). Existing /ui/api/archive* endpoints (GET list, restore, delete-from-archive) stay as backing handlers for one release as a soft-deprecate, then retire. Subsumes the originally-spec'd Mark Inactive flag (no new YAML field, no sidecar JSON) — Archive is the inactive state, since the only meaningful difference between them was "where the file lives," which is an implementation detail rather than a user feature; the trade-off accepted here is that compiling an archived device requires Unarchive → Compile → re-Archive (3 clicks for a rare action) rather than working in place. Tests: tests/test_scanner.py round-trip for scan_archived; /ui/api/devices shape test asserting the merged archived field on every row; poller eviction test asserting _devices no longer contains the device after the archive POST returns; ha-addon/ui/e2e/archived-toggle.spec.ts covering the column-picker entry, archived-row opacity + reduced action menu, Unarchive flipping back to active, and Permanently-delete closing the row entirely.

title: CI.5 PY-10b skipped-test ratio invariant
description: PY-10b landed. scripts/check-invariants.sh now counts @pytest.mark.skip decorators vs def test_ / async def test_ declarations across every non-_logic tests/test_integration_*.py file; fails CI if the ratio crosses 50 %. Current state: 0 skips across 11 honest tests in test_integration_reauth_flow.py + test_integration_reconfigure_flow.py + test_integration_setup.py → ratio is 0 %. Documented in CLAUDE.md § Enforced Invariants alongside PY-10. Total invariant count went 16 → 17.

title: CI.3 compile-test.yml matrices on esphome_version
description: compile-test.yml now matrices on esphome_version: ["2026.4.0", "2026.4.2"] for both compile-client (16 targets) and compile-server (4 targets). The pinned floor (2026.4.0 — same as MIN_ESPHOME_VERSION in constants.py, kept in sync via a workflow-level comment) anchors reproducibility; the latest-stable axis catches upstream API regressions of the kind from 1.5 ("2026.4.0 reshaped the API"). PlatformIO cache key now keys on ${{ matrix.esphome_version }} so the two axes don't poison each other's cache, and the per-step name surfaces the matrix combo (Compile esp32-idf on ESPHome 2026.4.2) so a red square in the GitHub UI tells the user which axis broke at a glance. The hardcoded env.ESPHOME_VERSION is gone — bumping the latest-stable axis is now a one-line edit to the matrix.

title: HT.6 ha-addon/ui/e2e/connect-worker-modal.spec.ts — 4 specs
description: ha-addon/ui/e2e/connect-worker-modal.spec.ts landed with 4 specs. Renders the modal, walks each format tab (bash / powershell / compose) via a commandFor(page, format) helper, then asserts the load-bearing shape per branch: bash + powershell carry --network host, -e SERVER_URL=…, and -v esphome-versions:/esphome-versions; compose carries network_mode: host, the SERVER_URL= env line, and the esphome-versions:/esphome-versions named-volume mount with no docker run prefix. A fourth spec asserts the live SERVER_URL value (http://localhost:8765 from the mocked serverInfo fixture) appears verbatim across every format. All 4 pass locally.

title: HT.5 tests/test_mdns_advertiser.py — 7 tests
description: tests/test_mdns_advertiser.py landed with 7 tests. Covers: happy-path register/unregister against a mocked AsyncZeroconf (asserts ServiceInfo carries the right addresses / port / properties / server); _primary_ipv4() returning None → register skipped, stop() remains a no-op (PR #80's fix); socket.gethostname() returning 'localhost' → server=None (PR #80's fallback); stop() before start() is a safe no-op; start() twice — pinned as "non-idempotent, two register calls" so a future caller-side bug surfaces here rather than silently double-advertising; register-time exception → _info clears so stop() stays safe; unregister-time exception is swallowed. All 7 pass locally.

title: QS.G10 manifest minimum-HA-version field
description: WONTFIX (attempted 1.7.0-dev.27, reverted dev.28) — the workitem's premise (set "homeassistant": "2024.11.0" in manifest.json) was wrong: that key is only allowed in core integration manifests. Custom-integration manifests reject it ("Invalid manifest: extra keys not allowed @ data['homeassistant']" — failed Hassfest in dev.27). There's no manifest-level field to declare a minimum HA version for a custom integration; the constraint is documented in DOCS.md and enforced at runtime via API-shape checks. The "document the minimum in DOCS.md" half folds into QS.G3 in 1.7.1's known-limitations sweep.

title: HT.15 Automate config-entry creation on the haos-pve test VM
description: Discovered 1.6.2-pre-tag while broadening @requires-ha coverage to haos-pve in commit 382e2b8: the integration installer copies files to /config/custom_components/esphome_fleet/ at add-on boot, but no automated step completes the config flow, so HA never registers esphome_fleet.compile / cancel / validate services. Result: the three ha-services.spec.ts specs (#64) returned 400 against haos-pve — not a 1.6.2 regression, just a test-infrastructure gap on the throwaway VM. Workaround landed: tagged the affected describe block with @requires-integration-config and --grep-invert-filtered it on haos-pve in scripts/test-matrix.py. The proper fix is to add a step to scripts/haos/install-addon.sh (or a new helper invoked from push-to-haos.sh) that POSTs /api/config/config_entries/flow with domain=esphome_fleet, walks async_step_user with base_url + the add-on token sourced from ~/.config/distributed-esphome/haos-addon-token, and confirms the entry reaches loaded state before Playwright kicks off. Once that lands, drop --grep-invert=@requires-integration-config from the haos-pve target so all @requires-ha specs run there too.

title: HT.12 Integration coverage measurement
description: Add --cov=ha-addon/custom_integration/esphome_fleet to the pytest invocation in pytest.ini (or pyproject.toml, wherever coverage config currently lives). Pipe to --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=95 guarded by an env var so local runs don't fail on intermediate states — CI sets the env var and gates on the threshold. Once this lands, the real number (post-HT.1 + HT.7 + HT.11) should be comfortably above 95%; confirm and flip test-coverage to done in quality_scale.yaml (closes QS.S1).

title: HT.10 Protocol cross-version mismatch test
description: Pin the current ha-addon/server/protocol.py as tests/fixtures/protocol_v{PROTOCOL_VERSION}.py at the start of the release cycle. New test in tests/test_protocol.py: instantiate a worker-shaped request-builder from the pinned old copy; POST it through the current server; assert graceful ProtocolError with a version-mismatch field (no undefined-field crash, no silent parse-as-unrelated-endpoint). PY-6 invariant covers 'server + client files byte-identical'; this covers 'we didn't break wire compat without bumping PROTOCOL_VERSION.'

title: HT.9 One stress test for firmware-storage concurrency
description: tests/test_firmware_storage.py gains: 10 concurrent firmware uploads via asyncio.gather against a single DAO with a budget set lower than the aggregate size. Assert: none get evicted mid-write (no half-written .bin files survive), budget enforcer's 'evict oldest' picks the correct victim under contention, has_firmware protection against coalesced-job eviction holds. Module took 81 new lines in 1.6.1 #9; current test file is 142 lines — thin.

title: HT.8 One stress test for git-versioning concurrency
description: tests/test_git_versioning.py gains: 50 concurrent commit_file calls via asyncio.gather against a single tmp repo. Assert 50 commits land in git log --oneline | wc -l, no .git/index.lock error, no file-content bleed across commits (e.g. commit N's content appears in commit N+1's tree). Module docstring explicitly flags the .git/index.lock race as a concern; there's currently no test that would detect if the module-level lock broke. If it passes today, baseline regression guard; if it fails, we have a real bug to fix.

title: HT.4 e2e-hass-4/static-ip-ota.spec.ts — prod regression guard
description: Add a target with wifi.manual_ip.static_ip: 192.0.2.1 (TEST-NET-1, unroutable by design). Trigger compile. Assert the resulting job record has ota_address == '192.0.2.1' (not shopaccesscontrol.local or similar). Compile fails at the OTA step because the IP is unroutable — intentional; the assertion is on job metadata, not successful upload. The static-IP bug has shipped twice (radiowave911 in 1.4.x and again in 1.6). A third ship is unacceptable; this guard forces the failure mode onto CI instead of into the next support thread. Sibling e2e-hass-4/wifi-domain-ota.spec.ts for #84 per the coverage plan: target with wifi.domain: .invalid-tld.test — compile succeeds, job record's ota_address ends in .invalid-tld.test, OTA fails at the resolve step.

title: HT.3 Static-IP fixture suite (the deferred-in-#18 trap)
description: tests/fixtures/esphome_configs/ gains: wifi_use_address.yaml, wifi_static_ip.yaml, ethernet_static_ip.yaml, openthread_use_address.yaml, wifi_static_ip_via_substitution.yaml (static_ip: ${ip} + substitutions block), wifi_static_ip_via_secret.yaml (static_ip: !secret my_ip), packages_with_network.yaml (address comes from an included package). New tests/test_ota_address_resolution.py parametrises over every fixture and asserts (address, source) matches what ESPHome's own esphome.core.CORE.address produces against the same YAML — ESPHome as the oracle, not hand-coded expected values, so the test tracks upstream behaviour automatically when ESPHome's resolver shifts. Also folds in wifi_domain.yaml, ethernet_domain.yaml, wifi_domain_via_substitution.yaml, wifi_domain_via_secret.yaml per #84's coverage plan.

title: HT.2 Reseed-consumer invariant (check-invariants.sh new rule)
description: The class of bug behind #11 (1.6.1) (encryption-key race on fresh boot) and #18 (1.6.1) (static-IP OTA regression) is the same: _resolve_esphome_config returns None during the ESPHome lazy-install window, leaving _encryption_keys / _address_overrides / _name_map unseeded. Fix landed as main.reseed_device_poller_from_config. New invariant: grep for every module-level read of those three dicts; for each hit, require the same module references reseed_device_poller_from_config OR is main.py itself. Fails CI if a future consumer lands without the reseed wire-up. This is the durable close on the bug class — don't skip it in favour of yet another narrow test.

title: QS.G8 repair-issues — audit actionable conditions, add custom issues
description: quality_scale.yaml:repair-issues says done because ConfigEntryAuthFailed auto-creates a repair. That's one condition; Gold wants us to surface every user-actionable condition via ir.async_create_issue. Audit for these: (a) Worker offline >1h despite being configured → fixable by starting/restarting the worker or removing the config. Severity WARNING. (b) Firmware-storage budget full → fixable by clearing old binaries or raising the budget. Severity WARNING. Link to the Queue-History dialog's Download tab. (c) ESPHome lazy-install failed (PyPI unreachable, no disk space) → surface the install error as a repair issue with the stderr blob. Severity ERROR. (d) Scheduled upgrade failed three times in a row for the same target → Severity WARNING; fix hint: check device reachability or pinned-version mismatch. (e) Worker self-paused on disk pressure (Worker.health_blocked_reason == 'disk_full', landed in 1.7.0 #219). The server-side gate already keeps work off a full worker; what's missing on the HA side is a Repair card so a non-admin user notices that the fleet is partially degraded without having to open the Workers tab. Severity WARNING; description includes the disk-used-pct from the worker's last heartbeat; fix hint: open the Workers tab → Actions → Clean cache, or grow the worker's disk. Self-clears the moment the registry reports health_blocked_reason = None (i.e. usage dropped back below the 90% exit threshold) — wire the ir.async_delete_issue to the same coordinator update tick that detects the recovery. For each, define the issue in strings.json → issues.<issue_id> with title + description, create on detection, clear with ir.async_delete_issue when the condition resolves. Non-actionable noise stays in logs — don't pollute Repairs with transient events. Update the quality_scale.yaml:repair-issues comment to enumerate the new issues.

title: QS.G7 stale-devices — add async_remove_config_entry_device
description: Current state: stale-devices is active removal via registry.async_remove_device in __init__.py:226 when the coordinator's target/worker snapshot drops an entry. That closes the automatic side of the rule, but HA's device page also offers a per-device Delete button whose enablement requires the integration to define async def async_remove_config_entry_device(hass, config_entry, device_entry) -> bool at the top level of __init__.py. Without it, the Delete button is greyed-out and users can't clear stale devices manually (e.g. a worker that's been physically decommissioned but the server still remembers). Implement it: return True when the device's identifier no longer appears in the coordinator snapshot; False otherwise (still active — refuse). Covered by a unit test in tests/test_integration_remove_device_logic.py. Update the quality_scale.yaml:stale-devices comment to name both the active-removal and user-removal paths.

title: QS.G6 runtime-data — migrate to entry.runtime_data
description: The quality_scale.yaml:109-115 comment hedged 'migration planned when HA minimum is bumped past 2024.11' — we're well past (today is 2026-04). 1.6.2's TP.3 restated the hedge honestly; 1.7.1 actually migrates. Concretely: (1) Replace hass.data[DOMAIN][entry.entry_id] = coordinator in __init__.py with entry.runtime_data = coordinator. (2) Update every platform read: sensor.py:56, binary_sensor.py, button.py, number.py, update.py — replace hass.data[DOMAIN][entry.entry_id] with entry.runtime_data. (3) Introduce a typed ConfigEntry alias: type EsphomeFleetConfigEntry = ConfigEntry[EsphomeFleetCoordinator] in const.py or a new types.py; annotate async_setup_entry / async_unload_entry / platform setups / diagnostics / config_flow async_get_options_flow to use it. (This also pre-pays for Platinum's strict-typing rule, whose runtime-data validator adds typed-alias checks when strict-typing is done.) (4) Update diagnostics.py to read via entry.runtime_data. (5) Audit hass.data cleanup in async_unload_entry: since there's nothing there to clean up post-migration, remove the pop. (6) Run full test + hass-4 smoke; flip runtime-data to done in quality_scale.yaml.

title: QS.G5 entity-translations — move every _attr_name to _attr_translation_key
description: Current state: zero entities use _attr_translation_key (verified by grep -c _attr_translation_key ha-addon/custom_integration/esphome_fleet/{sensor,binary_sensor,button,number,update}.py → all 0). Every entity ships an English-only name via _attr_name = '…'. Work: (1) Enumerate every distinct entity shape across the five platforms — target scheduled-upgrade sensor, worker online binary_sensor, worker clean-cache button, worker parallel-slots number, target update entity, etc. Give each a short snake-case translation key. (2) Replace _attr_name = 'Queue depth' → _attr_translation_key = 'queue_depth' (and drop _attr_name — HA composes from entity.<platform>.queue_depth.name in strings.json). (3) Populate strings.json → entity.sensor.queue_depth.name, etc., for every key. Mirror to translations/en.json. (4) For entities whose device_class already provides a translated name (the built-in rule exemption — binary_sensor/number/sensor/update with a device_class set), verify the name shows up correctly without a translation_key and note the exemption in the entity's code comment. (5) Verify in the HA UI: entity names render identically to today; Customize dialog shows the English names as defaults and exposes them for localization. (6) Flip entity-translations to done in quality_scale.yaml.

title: QS.G4 docs-troubleshooting — single dedicated section
description: Consolidate into ## Troubleshooting with the symptom→cause→fix shape the gold rule wants: 'Integration card says Reconfigure' → token rotated or URL changed → run Reconfigure flow; 'Entities stuck at unavailable' → add-on URL mismatch or add-on stopped → check Supervisor logs + URL; 'Zeroconf discovery never fires on a fresh HA' → mDNS reflector not enabled on the router, add-on URL must be entered manually; 'Reauth flow dead-ends' → expired refresh-token path, delete + re-add entry (rare; 1.6.2's TR.6 closed a code-path contributor). Four to six items is enough; refresh as real support threads surface.

title: QS.G3 docs-known-limitations — single dedicated section
description: Consolidate what's scattered across DOCS.md today into a ## Known limitations section: (a) HA Core restart required after integration-code upgrade (Python module caching); (b) Supervisor @sha256: digest pinning blocked on upstream Supervisor schema; (c) AppArmor profile is first-pass confinement only (narrow denies on secrets + /proc/*/mem + /sys/kernel writes, unrestricted file/network elsewhere) — link to SECURITY.md for the threat model; (d) worker-offline detection uses a 30s heartbeat window; transient blips of ~45s register as offline-then-online; (e) the factory-vs-OTA firmware-variant distinction isn't surfaced in the integration's Update entity — users pick in the Web UI; (f) HA-version floor: this integration requires HA Core ≥ 2024.11 — declared here rather than in manifest.json because custom-integration manifests reject the homeassistant key (see the QS.G10 disposition in dev-plans/archive/WORKITEMS-1.7.0.md); the constraint is enforced at runtime via API-shape checks, and HA <2024.11 will surface an unhelpful traceback rather than a clean message.

title: QS.G2 docs-examples — formal Examples section
description: DOCS.md → Integration currently sketches automations informally. Restructure into a ## Examples section with at least three concrete scenarios, each as a copy-pasteable YAML snippet that references our entities: (i) fire a notification when any target's Update entity reports a pending version, (ii) trigger the esphome_fleet.compile service on schedule via HA Scheduler, (iii) route a worker-offline binary-sensor transition to a dashboard warning card. Link at least one to a published HA blueprint if we author one; otherwise note that blueprint contributions are welcome.

title: QS.G1 docs-data-update — Integration DOCS section
description: Add a 'How data updates' subsection to ha-addon/DOCS.md → Integration. Explain: coordinator polls the add-on's /ui/api/* endpoints every 30s (update_interval=timedelta(seconds=30) in coordinator.py); a push WebSocket supplements the poll for real-time event signals; the user can force an immediate refresh via the integration card's Reload button. Flip the quality_scale.yaml:docs-data-update entry to done when the section is live.

title: QS.S1 Silver test-coverage → Gold-grade coverage
description: Silver's bar is ≥95% real line-coverage (not mocked). 1.6.2 landed HT.1 / HT.7 / HT.11 (real-hass lifecycle + reconfigure + reauth flow tests) which unblocks most of this; HT.12 (coverage measurement) still needs to land here. Sequence: HT.12 lands → re-run pytest --cov=ha-addon.custom_integration.esphome_fleet → confirm ≥95% → flip test-coverage to done in quality_scale.yaml. Until then it stays todo and Gold doesn't ship. Chain: QS.S1 ⇐ HT.12 ⇐ CI.6 (gate).

title: QS.B1 Submit brand assets to home-assistant/brands
description: Bronze rule. Artwork is staged under docs/brands-submission/ (per quality_scale.yaml:35-40); the PR to home-assistant/brands hasn't been opened. Prepare the submission (matching that repo's README: icon.png 256x256, icon@2x.png 512x512, logo.png, logo@2x.png — all under custom_integrations/esphome_fleet/), open the PR, link it back here. This rule can ship as done in our file once the brands PR is merged; until then, leave it todo with the PR URL in the comment so it's visible why Gold's on hold.

title: UD.5 ESP column renamed Platform with chip + board lines
description: Existing #23 "ESP" column on the Devices tab is now a stacked two-line cell renamed "Platform": chip family on the primary line (ESP32, ESP32-S3, ESP8266, RP2040, Host) and PlatformIO board on a smaller muted secondary line (esp32dev, esp32-s3-devkitm-1, d1_mini, rpipico, …). Server side: scanner._extract_metadata reads board from inside esp32: / esp8266: / rp2040: blocks (Host has no board concept); board field added to the metadata dict default + /ui/api/targets payload (both managed + archived rows). Defaults to None when the YAML failed to resolve or doesn't pick a platform. Sort key remains the bare chip-family string so ESP32 / ESP32-S3 / ESP8266 group naturally; board comes along within each chip family. Tooltip text widened to "Chip family + PlatformIO board from the resolved YAML: <chip> on <board>" when both are present. Tests: 6 new in tests/test_scanner.py cover board extraction from each chip block, the ESP32-S3 variant + board pair, the no-board fallback, and the Host-no-board case. ha-addon/ui/e2e/platform-column.spec.ts (4 specs) covers the renamed header, the two-line render for ESP32-S3 + ESP8266 rows, and the muted em-dash fallback when neither field is set. The future "read from storage.json post-compile" path is deferred — the YAML round-trip already covers the practical case (bug #23's resolution path runs ESPHome's full validator, so the values are authoritative); a storage.json source-of-truth would only matter for diagnosing build-time discrepancies, which can be filed separately if it ever bites.

title: UD.4 Bluetooth-proxy column on Devices tab
description: SUBSUMED-BY-#23 (1.7.0-dev.18 / closed in dev.27) — Bluetooth-proxy column landed earlier as part of bug #23's "two new optional Devices-tab columns" patch. The `BLE proxy` column at useDeviceColumns.tsx:452 renders Active (success-green) / Passive / — (off) with the same column-picker toggle the workitem specced. Sourced from Target.bluetooth_proxy ('off' | 'passive' | 'active') populated by scanner._extract_metadata's bluetooth_proxy: block detection. The EH.3 pairing note (use CONF_BLUETOOTH_PROXY once the magic-string sweep lands) folds into EH.3's text — no separate work here.

title: UD.3 reconfigure form submit button reads Save changes
description: Reconfigure form's submit button now reads "Save changes" via the per-step "submit" translation key (added to strings.json + translations/en.json's reconfigure_confirm block) instead of HA's stock "Submit". Per the UX_REVIEW-1.6.1 §4.4 disposition. Other steps (user, discovery_confirm, reauth_confirm) keep HA's default for now — QS.G5's translation pass will sweep them.

title: UD.2 worker-selection column responsive labels
description: Worker-selection column now renders a short label below the Tailwind xl: breakpoint (1280 px) and the long label at ≥1280 px. formatSelectionReason (utils/selectionReason.ts) gains a shortLabel field per case (Pinned to worker → Pinned, Only worker online → Only online, Only eligible by tag → Only eligible, Least busy worker → Least busy, Fastest worker available → Fastest); the cell wraps both spans in `hidden xl:inline` / `xl:hidden` so the visible form swaps purely via CSS — no JS resize listener, no React state. Same change applied to the matching column in QueueHistoryDialog so the two surfaces don't drift. Tooltip retains the long context regardless of viewport. Tests: 4 specs in ha-addon/ui/e2e/worker-selection-responsive.spec.ts cover both viewports, the Pinned variant, and the always-on tooltip — protect against future regressions where someone reverts to a single label or moves the breakpoint. Origin: UX_REVIEW-1.6.1 UX.2 — long form ("Fastest worker available") overflowed the cell on a standard 13" laptop.

title: UD.1 per-source explanatory tooltip on address-source label
description: Per-source explanatory tooltip on the address-source label under the Devices-tab IP cell. New formatAddressSourceTooltip helper mirrored across useDeviceColumns.tsx (managed-row IP) and DevicesTab.tsx (unmanaged-row IP), returning a sentence per source ("Detected via ARP scan of the local network — mDNS came up empty…", "From wifi.use_address in the device YAML — overrides hostname-based discovery", etc.). Replaces the old `Address source: <raw_enum>` title that surfaced the API string. The ha_configured suffix on the unmanaged row picks up the new sentence too.

title: QS.P1 Scope strict-typing for a future release
description: Run mypy --strict against ha-addon/custom_integration/esphome_fleet/ and count the diagnostics; triage into (a) genuinely fixable right here (add an annotation), (b) fixable after QS.G6 lands (typed ConfigEntry alias unlocks half of them), (c) bounded by Any on coordinator dict reads — needs a TypedDict for the server's response shape (which is pydantic-shaped on the server side; we could import and re-use the protocol.py models). No code changes this release — but produce a short dev-plans/STRICT-TYPING-PLAN.md that enumerates counts, categories, and a 1.8 or 1.9 landing plan. Platinum also needs every dep in manifest.json.requirements to ship py.typed or a types-* stub; since our requirements list is empty, that half is free. Update the quality_scale.yaml:strict-typing comment with the counts from the audit.

title: QS.G9 entity-disabled-by-default — re-audit the 'exempt — all useful' claim
description: quality_scale.yaml:309-313 currently reads exempt. That may or may not be right; the rule wants niche or high-cardinality entities disabled by default. Audit every entity and decide — _attr_entity_registry_enabled_default = False on anything whose value (i) changes more often than every ~5 minutes on a steady-state fleet, or (ii) is only useful for debugging. Candidates: per-worker active-jobs count (high-churn), the scheduled_once sensor (rarely consulted). If after the audit no entity qualifies, keep exempt but replace the comment with the audit rationale ('audited every entity at <date>; none qualify as niche/noisy'). If any qualify, mark done and list them.

title: EH.4 Simplify ESPHome version detection
description: ha-addon/server/scanner.py:207-228 (_get_installed_esphome_version) shells to <venv>/bin/esphome version and string-parses 'Version: X.Y.Z' on stdout as the primary path; importlib.metadata.version('esphome') at lines 232-234 is a fallback. Once the venv's site-packages is on sys.path (which happens before _esphome_ready fires) the two paths return the same answer — the subprocess is redundant and ~50ms of fork+exec on a hot path. Fix: either (a) reorder so importlib.metadata is primary and subprocess is the disambiguator when we have reason to believe server-process Python is pointing at a different ESPHome than the venv, or (b) import esphome.const.__version__ directly (single attr read, no subprocess, no parsing). Keep the current memoization. Lowest priority of the three; bundle with any nearby scanner.py work rather than as a standalone PR. Test: tests/test_scanner_version.py with and without the venv activated, asserting the two paths agree and the subprocess is skipped on the warm path. Carryover from 1.7.0.

title: EH.3 Replace magic-string config keys with `esphome.const` imports
description: ha-addon/server/scanner.py and ha-addon/client/client.py reference 'esphome', 'name', 'wifi', 'ethernet', 'openthread', 'api', 'substitutions', 'packages', 'platform', 'framework', 'board', 'use_address', 'manual_ip', 'static_ip', 'domain' as string literals scattered across many call sites. ESPHome's own code uses CONF_ESPHOME, CONF_NAME, CONF_WIFI, CONF_ETHERNET, CONF_OPENTHREAD, CONF_API, CONF_SUBSTITUTIONS, CONF_PACKAGES, CONF_PLATFORM, CONF_FRAMEWORK, CONF_BOARD, CONF_USE_ADDRESS, CONF_MANUAL_IP, CONF_STATIC_IP, CONF_DOMAIN from esphome.const — any upstream rename becomes an ImportError in our layer instead of a silent dict-miss that drops friendly_name / use_address / etc. for every user. Mechanical sweep; ruff + mypy + existing test suite catches typos. Scope the change to files where ESPHome is already on sys.path (i.e. anywhere touched after _esphome_ready fires) — the cold-start fallback paths keep the literal strings so they work before the venv is installed. UD.4's pairing note (use CONF_BLUETOOTH_PROXY once the magic-string sweep lands) folds in here — UD.4 itself shipped in 1.7.0-dev.18 via bug #23. Carryover from 1.7.0.

title: EH.1 Use `esphome idedata` for firmware-artifact discovery
description: ha-addon/client/client.py:1225-1275 (_collect_firmware_variants) walks .esphome/build/<name>/.pioenvs/<name>/firmware.{factory,}.bin via hardcoded path templates. Stable for ESP32/ESP8266 today; breaks the moment ESPHome ships a target platform with a different PlatformIO layout (RP2040, nRF52, Zephyr, the new host platform). ESPHome's esphome idedata <yaml> (and esphome.platformio_api.get_idedata() Python API) emits JSON including firmware_elf_path, firmware_bin_path, and extra_flash_images (list of {offset, path} entries for bootloader/partition-table blobs). Fix: after a successful esphome run, invoke <venv>/bin/esphome idedata <yaml> and parse the JSON as the authoritative artifact manifest; retain today's path walk as a legacy fallback (probe once per job, cache the decision). Bonus: extra_flash_images unblocks a future USB-first-flash flow without further reinvention (currently we only archive firmware.factory.bin + firmware.bin; bootloader + partition table are silently dropped). Test: tests/test_client_firmware_collection.py across ESP32 (factory+ota), ESP8266 (ota only), and a synthetic RP2040 fixture; mocked idedata JSON drives the parse path and a real compile against cyd-office-info proves the wire. Carryover from 1.7.0.
